itinerary.callout: Route: Fly into Kutaisi → Black Sea coast (Batumi) → west Georgia nature → south Georgia (Vardzia) → Tbilisi → Kazbegi mountains → wine country → cross into Armenia → Yerevan day trips → fly home from Yerevan. Zero car rental. All public transport.

items.description: Sat departures. Airport shuttle: Georgian Bus 16 GEL or taxi 30–35 GEL into city.
items.title: Wizz Air PRG → KUT direct (~3h 35min)

items.description: 11th-century ruined cathedral on a hilltop with sweeping valley views. Short taxi ~5 GEL from centre.
items.title: Bagrati Cathedral (UNESCO)

items.description: Georgia's finest medieval complex — gold mosaics, royal tombs, 12th-century frescoes. 10km from city. Marshrutka from bus station or taxi ~10 GEL return.
items.title: Gelati Monastery (UNESCO)

items.description: Imeretian khachapuri, khinkali dumplings, local wine. Canteen meal 15–25 GEL (~€5–9).
items.title: Dinner in Kutaisi Old Town

items.description: Communal Hotel (mid-range) or family guesthouses. Book ahead for July peak.
items.title: Overnight: Kutaisi — 2 nights

days.subtitle: Bagrati Cathedral · Gelati · Old Town dinner
days.title: Arrive Kutaisi — UNESCO Monasteries

items.description: Taxi to Kutaisi Airport Station (30 min). Fast train, book at railway.ge or buy at station. Alt: Georgian Bus coach from Chavchavadze Ave, ~2.5 hrs, 30 GEL — no booking needed.
items.title: Fast train: Kutaisi Airport Station → Batumi (~1h 50min)

items.description: Palm-lined seafront with sculptures, fountains, cafes, and pebbly beach. Ali & Nino kinetic sculpture is the most-photographed spot. Swim in July — water is warm. Completely free.
items.title: Batumi Boulevard — 7km Black Sea promenade

items.description: 19th-century facades mixing French, Italian and Ottoman styles, dramatically contrasting with the futuristic skyline. Alphabet Tower for city panorama.
items.title: Batumi Old Town + Europe Square

items.description: Batumi's signature — bread boat filled with cheese, topped with egg and butter, stirred tableside. The most iconic Georgian food experience.
items.title: Adjarian khachapuri lunch

items.description: Check return times ahead. Georgian Bus has evening departures. Don't miss the last service!
items.title: Return Batumi → Kutaisi (train or Georgian Bus)

days.subtitle: Train to coast · Boulevard · Adjarian khachapuri · swim
days.title: Day trip: Batumi & the Black Sea 🌊

items.description: Marshrutka 30: Kutaisi station → Tskaltubo (1.2 GEL, 30 min). Then Marshrutka 42 → Prometheus Cave (2 GEL, 15 min). Open Tue–Sun 10:00–18:00.
items.title: Kutaisi → Prometheus Cave via Tskaltubo

items.description: Georgia's largest cave — stalactites, stalagmites, underground river, optional boat. Join a guided group on arrival. Non-slip shoes essential. Allow 1.5 hrs.
items.title: Prometheus Cave (morning)

items.description: Back to Tskaltubo (2 GEL), marshrutka to Martvili town (4 GEL, ~1hr). Final 3km to canyon gate: local taxi 15–20 GEL — no public bus reaches the entrance. Canyon closed Mondays.
items.title: Prometheus → Martvili Canyon

items.description: Turquoise gorge with 700m riverside walk and 20-min boat ride. One of Georgia's most viral spots. Go after 14:00 to avoid tour groups for better photos.
items.title: Martvili Canyon (afternoon)

items.description: Taxi to town (15 GEL), marshrutka back (~4 GEL). Last marshrutka ~17:00 — leave canyon by 15:30!
items.title: Return Martvili → Kutaisi

days.subtitle: Marshrutkas + 3km taxi to canyon gate
days.title: Day trip: Prometheus Cave + Martvili Canyon

items.description: Departs 08:20, 09:30, 11:30, 13:00. Take the 08:20! Journey ~3.5 hrs via Borjomi. Cost: 12–17 GEL.
items.title: Kutaisi Bus Station → Akhaltsikhe (marshrutka)

items.description: Impressive Ottoman-era fortress right next to the bus station. 30-min walkabout while waiting for the Vardzia bus.
items.title: Akhaltsikhe — Rabati Fortress (brief stop)

items.description: Buy ticket inside bus station. Departs 10:30, 12:20, 16:00. Journey 1.5 hrs, 6.50–7 GEL. ⚠️ Last return bus from Vardzia ~15:00 — strongly consider overnighting near Vardzia.
items.title: Akhaltsikhe → Vardzia (marshrutka)

items.description: 12th-century cave city: 13 storeys cut into volcanic cliffs, 3,000 rooms, frescoes, ancient wine cellar. One of Georgia's absolute highlights. Free internal shuttle bus within the complex. Allow 2–3 hrs.
items.title: Vardzia Cave Monastery

items.description: Valodias Cottages near Vardzia (~75 GEL incl. dinner) or hotels in Akhaltsikhe town.
items.title: Overnight near Vardzia or Akhaltsikhe

days.subtitle: 12th-century cave city of Queen Tamar
days.title: Kutaisi → Akhaltsikhe → Vardzia

items.description: ~3 hrs, 15 GEL. Arrives at Didube Bus Station. Ask for a Mtskheta drop-off (30 min before Tbilisi) or take a separate Didube→Mtskheta marshrutka (1.5 GEL, 20 min).
items.title: Akhaltsikhe → Tbilisi (marshrutka, hourly from ~06:20)

items.description: Jvari on a clifftop (6th century, views of two rivers). Svetitskhoveli Cathedral (11th century, Georgia's holiest site). 2–3 hrs.
items.title: Mtskheta — Jvari Monastery + Svetitskhoveli (UNESCO)

items.description: Narikala Fortress (free city panorama), Abanotubani sulfur district with domed rooftops, Gabriadze Theatre clock tower, wooden-balconied houses of Sololaki.
items.title: Tbilisi Old Town afternoon

items.description: Natural hot spring baths since the 5th century — a Tbilisi institution. Public from 10 GEL/hr. Private rooms ~50 GEL/hr.
items.title: Sulfur bath experience

items.description: Fabrika Hostel (best social atmosphere in Georgia) or guesthouses in Sololaki Old Town.
items.title: Overnight: Tbilisi — 2 nights

days.subtitle: UNESCO ancient capital · Old Town · Sulfur baths
days.title: South Georgia → Tbilisi via Mtskheta

items.description: Bus station next to Samgori metro exit. Every ~2 hrs from 09:00. Journey ~2–2.5 hrs, 10 GEL. Returns: 07:00, 09:00, 11:00, 13:00, 16:00, 18:00. Book return at the kiosk immediately on arrival — July fills up!
items.title: Tbilisi Samgori Metro → Sighnaghi (marshrutka, day trip)

items.description: 18th-century walled hilltop town with 23 towers and views over the Alazani Valley. Walk the 4.5km city walls for free. One of Georgia's most Instagrammed towns.
items.title: Sighnaghi — City of Love

items.description: Georgia's finest natural wine producer right in town. Traditional qvevri amber wines with local cheese and churchkhela (walnut-grape candy).
items.title: Wine tasting — Pheasant's Tears winery

items.description: Tbilisi Funicular to Mtatsminda Park (~4 GEL) for city panorama. Then Fabrika courtyard bars or Tbilisi's renowned electronic music clubs.
items.title: Tbilisi evening: Funicular + drinks

days.subtitle: Wine country · City of Love · Funicular · Nightlife
days.title: Tbilisi + day trip: Sighnaghi

items.description: Metro to Didube station. Journey ~3.5 hrs, 15 GEL. Arrive 30 min early in July. Ask driver for a photo stop at Ananuri Fortress.
items.title: Tbilisi Didube → Stepantsminda (marshrutka, hourly 08:00–19:00)

items.description: Medieval fortress on a lake reservoir — most drivers pause 10–15 min. Great reflection shots.
items.title: Ananuri Fortress (marshrutka photo stop)

items.description: Georgia's most photographed place — 14th-century stone church at 2,170m with Mt. Kazbek (5,047m) behind it. Hike up 1.5–2 hrs (steep) or 4x4 taxi ~20–30 GEL. Stay overnight for the sunrise shot.
items.title: Gergeti Trinity Church

items.description: Guesthouses usually include dinner. Wake at 05:30 for the sunrise over Mt. Kazbek.
items.title: Overnight: Stepantsminda

days.subtitle: Georgian Military Highway · Gergeti Trinity Church
days.title: Tbilisi → Kazbegi — Overnight in the mountains

items.description: 15 GEL, 3.5 hrs. Head to Avlabari area on arrival.
items.title: Stepantsminda → Tbilisi (marshrutka, hourly ~08:00)

items.description: Corner of Dusheti & Armazi St, near Avlabari metro. Departs ~09:00, 11:00, 14:00. Journey 5–6 hrs incl. border. Cost ~$20 USD cash. ⚠️ Pre-book 1 day ahead in July! Ask driver to stop at Haghpat monastery.
items.title: Tbilisi (Avlabari) → Yerevan (shared minibus)

items.description: UNESCO 10th–13th century monastery above the Debed River Canyon. Many drivers stop if asked ahead. Combine with Sanahin (5 min away).
items.title: Haghpat Monastery (ask driver to stop)

items.description: Stay near Republic Square for best access to everything.
items.title: Overnight: Yerevan — 3 nights

days.subtitle: Haghpat UNESCO stop · Arrive Yerevan evening
days.title: Kazbegi → Tbilisi → Cross into Armenia

items.description: Take bus 46/261 from Yeritasardakan metro to Northern Bus Station. Marshrutkas every 30–60 min from 07:00. Journey ~1.5 hrs, 700–800 AMD. Return by 18:30 latest.
items.title: Yerevan Northern Bus Station → Lake Sevan (marshrutka)

items.description: 9th-century monastery on a peninsula with panoramic lake views — 200+ steps up. One of Armenia's most photographed spots.
items.title: Sevanavank Monastery

items.description: Lake reaches ~20°C in July. All lakeside restaurants serve ishkhan (Sevan trout) — Armenia's prized local delicacy.
items.title: Swim + fresh Sevan trout lunch

days.subtitle: Marshrutka · Sevanavank monastery · Swim · Trout lunch
days.title: Day trip: Lake Sevan

items.description: Pink tuff-stone buildings, National Gallery, History Museum. Musical fountain shows at night.
items.title: Republic Square + city walk

items.description: 572-step limestone staircase with free outdoor sculptures and Ararat views from the top. Armenia's most viral urban spot. Best at sunset.
items.title: The Cascade

items.description: Trolleybus 3 or 5 from Republic Square (~100 AMD). Eternal flame + museum. Essential context for understanding Armenia.
items.title: Tsitsernakaberd — Armenian Genocide Memorial

items.description: Since 1887. Underground barrels from 1902, Churchill's favourite. Tour + 4 tastings. Walk or taxi 500 AMD.
items.title: Ararat Brandy Factory tour

days.subtitle: Republic Square · Cascade · Genocide Memorial · Ararat Brandy
days.title: Yerevan city day

items.description: 100 AMD metro. Bus 467 from Southern Bus Station: 09:00, 14:00, 18:00. Journey ~1 hr, 250 AMD. Walk 15 min from junction to monastery. Return: 07:23, 10:23, 15:23.
items.title: Metro → Sasuntsi Davit → Bus 467 → Khor Virap

items.description: The most iconic view in Armenia — Mt. Ararat rising behind the monastery. Go early morning for best visibility. Descend into the 6m pit dungeon where St. Gregory was imprisoned for 13 years.
items.title: Khor Virap Monastery

items.description: Bus 5, 8, 23 or 35 from centre to Gai Bus Station. Bus 266/284 to Garni village, 250–500 AMD, ~40 min. For Geghard: bus goes ~3km short — walk 3km or taxi ~500 AMD. Last bus from Garni back: ~19:46.
items.title: City bus to Gai Station → Bus 266/284 → Garni + Geghard

items.description: 1st-century pagan sun temple (only Greco-Roman colonnaded building in the former USSR). Walk down into Garni Gorge for the Symphony of Stones — natural hexagonal basalt columns, endlessly viral on social media.
items.title: Garni Temple + Symphony of Stones

items.description: Carved partly into living rock. Dark, atmospheric interior with carved khachkars and a spring inside the church. One of Armenia's most moving and viral travel destinations.
items.title: Geghard Monastery (UNESCO)

days.subtitle: All by public buses — under €4 transport total
days.title: Day trips: Khor Virap + Garni + Geghard

items.description: Bus 201 runs 24/7 from Yeritasardakan metro. ~35 min, 100 AMD. Or GG taxi ~2,500 AMD. Allow 2+ hrs before departure in July.
items.title: Yerevan → Zvartnots Airport (Bus 201 or GG taxi)

items.description: Book at wizzair.com. Mon/Wed/Fri departures.
items.title: Wizz Air EVN → PRG direct · Fri July 25 · ~4h 20min

items.description: Open-air flea market near Republic Square (Sat/Sun) — Soviet antiques, carpets, handmade crafts. Or a morning walk through Kond, Yerevan's oldest neighbourhood.
items.title: Optional morning: Vernissage Market

days.subtitle: Wizz Air EVN → PRG · ~4h 20min · Direct
days.title: Fly home: Yerevan → Prague

subtitle: Kutaisi → Black Sea → Mountains → Tbilisi → Yerevan
tagline: Caucasus adventure — ancient monasteries, mountain churches, wine country, and the Black Sea coast. All by public bus and marshrutka.

cards.body: Both countries are cash-heavy. Bring euros and withdraw local currency from ATMs on arrival — never exchange at airports. Use Revolut or Wise for fee-free ATM withdrawals. Georgia: Lari ~1 EUR = 2.85 GEL. Armenia: Dram ~1 EUR = 400 AMD.
cards.title: Money

cards.body: Buy at the airport. Georgia: Geocell or Magti, ~5 GEL for 10GB. Armenia: Ucom or Viva-MTS, ~1,000 AMD for 10GB. Essential for offline maps, Bolt/GG taxis, and bus timetable research.
cards.title: SIM Cards

cards.body: Bolt across Georgia. GG or Yandex Go in Armenia. Always use apps — street taxis regularly overcharge tourists 3–5x. Download both before leaving Prague.
cards.title: Taxi Apps

cards.body: No visa for EU/Czech passport in either country. Land border: bring passport (not just ID card). Budget 30–90 min at Sadakhlo/Bagratashen crossing. Open 24/7.
cards.title: Visas & Border

cards.body: Cities: 30–36°C, very sunny. Kazbegi: 15–22°C, cool. Batumi: warm but humid, afternoon thunderstorms possible. Pack light clothes + one warm layer for evenings/mountains. Sunscreen essential.
cards.title: July Weather

cards.body: Cover shoulders and knees at all monasteries and churches — both countries are deeply Christian. Women may need a headscarf; most monasteries provide one at the entrance. Quiet behaviour inside churches.
cards.title: Monastery Etiquette

cards.body: Local canteens: €2–4 for a full meal. Must-try Georgia: khachapuri, khinkali, mtsvadi, qvevri wine. Armenia: khorovats (BBQ), dolma, lavash, Armenian brandy, Sevan trout. Adjarian khachapuri in Batumi is essential.
cards.title: Food Tips

cards.body: Scarf for monasteries. Comfortable walking shoes (cobblestones everywhere). Daypack. Head torch for cave and fortress visits. Portable charger. Packable rain layer (western Georgia). Offline Google Maps — download all areas before you go.
cards.title: Packing

cards.body: Both countries are very safe — safer than most Western European cities for street crime. Normal urban caution applies. In Armenia: stay away from the Azerbaijan border zone. Get travel insurance covering hiking.
cards.title: Safety

cards.body: Now: Both Wizz Air flights. 6 weeks out: Kutaisi guesthouses, Kazbegi guesthouse. 2 weeks out: Ask Tbilisi guesthouse to book Yerevan minibus. On arrival: SIM card, taxi apps, ATM cash.
cards.title: Booking Order

tips.note:
🗓️ Perfect dates: Fly out Saturday July 14 (PRG→KUT) · Return Friday July 25 (EVN→PRG). Both are direct Wizz Air days. 11 nights total.

title: Georgia &

transport.callout: Every leg of this trip is covered by public transport. The one exception requiring advance booking is the Tbilisi→Yerevan shared minibus — reserve 1 day ahead in July. Everything else is walkup on the day.

steps.text: Exit arrivals. Find the Georgian Bus shuttle — timed to every flight. Forecourt of terminal.
Bus to Kutaisi city centre (~25 min). Cost: 16 GEL. Pay onboard by card or cash.
Alternative: Bolt taxi to centre ~30–40 GEL. Download Bolt before leaving Prague.
Metro to Yeritasardakan station. Find the SAS supermarket opposite — Bus 201 stop is there.
Bus 201 runs 24/7, every 20–30 min. Journey ~35 min. Cost: 100 AMD (~€0.25).
Or GG / Yandex taxi: ~2,000–3,000 AMD (~€5–7). Faster and more reliable.
Bolt taxi to Kutaisi Airport Station (23km, ~30 min). Free shuttle between airport terminal and railway station.
Fast train to Batumi: ~1h 50min. Book at railway.ge or buy at station. Cost: 20–30 GEL.
Batumi station is 4.5km from the Boulevard. Bolt taxi to centre ~5 GEL.
Departs from Chavchavadze Avenue, Kutaisi (Youth Park stop). Several times daily. Journey ~2.5 hrs. WiFi + USB charging on board.
Arrives Batumi Bus Station, Gogol St. Bolt taxi to boulevard ~5 GEL.
Kutaisi Main Station → Marshrutka 30 to Tskaltubo (1.2 GEL, 30 min).
Tskaltubo central square → Marshrutka 42 to Prometheus Cave (2 GEL, 15 min). Cave open Tue–Sun 10:00–18:00.
After cave: return to Tskaltubo (2 GEL). Marshrutka toward Martvili town (4 GEL, ~1 hr).
Final 3km to canyon gate: local taxi 15–20 GEL. No public bus reaches the entrance — this short taxi is unavoidable.
Return: taxi to Martvili town (15 GEL), marshrutka back to Kutaisi (~4 GEL). Leave canyon by 15:30 — last marshrutka ~17:00.

routes.subtitle: Marshrutkas + one short taxi
routes.title: Full day loop: Kutaisi → Prometheus → Martvili → Kutaisi

steps.text: Kutaisi Central Bus Station → Akhaltsikhe: departs 08:20, 09:30, 11:30, 13:00. Take 08:20! ~3.5 hrs, 12–17 GEL.
Akhaltsikhe Bus Station (buy ticket inside building) → Vardzia: 10:30, 12:20, 16:00. ~1.5 hrs, 6.50–7 GEL.
⚠️ Last return bus Vardzia→Akhaltsikhe: ~15:00. Overnight near Vardzia strongly recommended, or round-trip taxi 70–80 GEL.
Hourly from ~06:20. Journey ~3 hrs. Cost: 15 GEL. Arrives Didube Bus Station, Tbilisi.
Mtskheta stopover: Ask driver to drop you in Mtskheta. Visit Jvari + Svetitskhoveli, then marshrutka to Tbilisi (~1.5 GEL, 20 min).
Metro to Samgori station. Bus station is right outside. Look for the Sighnaghi (სიღნაღი) van.
Every ~2 hrs from 09:00 to 18:00. Cost: 10 GEL. Journey ~2–2.5 hrs.
Book return at the kiosk immediately on arrival in Sighnaghi — fills in July. Returns: 07:00, 09:00, 11:00, 13:00, 16:00, 18:00.
Metro to Didube station. Find marshrutka yard. Look for "Kazbegi" vans.
Hourly 08:00–19:00. Cost: 15 GEL. Arrive 30 min early in July. Ask for Ananuri Fortress photo stop.
In Stepantsminda: 4x4 taxis to Gergeti Church at the main square. ~20–30 GEL return. Or hike up 1.5–2 hrs.
Corner of Dusheti St & Armazi St, near Avlabari metro — informal Yerevan minibus hub.
Departs ~09:00, 11:00, 14:00. Journey 5–6 hrs incl. border crossing. Cost: ~$20 USD cash paid to driver.
⚠️ Pre-book 1 day ahead in July. Ask your guesthouse to phone and reserve — they know the drivers.
At the border: carry all luggage through customs. Passport required (not just ID card).
Bus 46 or 261 from Yeritasardakan metro to Northern Bus Station.
Marshrutkas to Sevan every 30–60 min from 07:00. Cost: 700–800 AMD. ~1.5 hrs. Get off at Sevan town.
Last return marshrutka ~18:30 — don't miss it!
Metro to Sasuntsi Davit (100 AMD). Southern Bus Station is behind the train station.
Bus 467: departs 09:00, 14:00, 18:00. Cost: 250 AMD. ~1 hr. Walk 15 min from junction to monastery.
Return times: 07:23, 10:23, 15:23. Take 10:23 to allow time for Garni/Geghard afternoon.
Bus 5, 8, 23, 26 or 35 from city centre to Gai Bus Station (Nor-Nork suburb).
Bus 266 or 284 to Garni village. 250–500 AMD, ~40 min. Garni Temple is at the bus stop.
For Geghard: bus drops you ~3km short in Gokht village. Walk 3km or taxi ~500 AMD the last stretch.
Return from Garni: same bus. Last bus ~19:46 (verify at t-armenia.com).
Metro: 2 lines. Cost: 1 GEL/ride. Key stations: Didube (Kazbegi buses), Avlabari (Armenia minibus), Samgori (Kakheti/Sighnaghi), Isani (Marjanishvili nightlife).
Bolt app: cheapest city taxis, 5–12 GEL for most rides. Download before arriving.
Metro: 1 line, 10 stations. Cost: 100 AMD (~€0.25). Key stations: Yeritasardakan (airport bus, Northern Bus Station), Sasuntsi Davit (Khor Virap bus), Zoravar Andranik.
GG or Yandex taxi apps: 500–1,500 AMD for city rides. Much cheaper and more reliable than street taxis.

transport.note:
Essential apps: Bolt (Georgia taxis) · GG + Yandex Go (Armenia taxis) · Google Maps (download offline) · t-armenia.com (Yerevan bus timetables) · railway.ge (Georgia train bookings) · citybus.ge (Georgian Bus coaches)

Marshrutka tips: Carry small change — drivers rarely break large notes. In July arrive 20–30 min early for popular routes. The Tbilisi→Yerevan minibus is the only leg requiring pre-booking.

viral.callout: Georgia is one of Europe's fastest-growing travel destinations on social media. These are the spots driving the most views on TikTok and Instagram Reels right now. Most are already on your route!
viral.note:
📱 Photography timing: Gergeti Church — sunrise 05:30–06:30, overnight required. Khor Virap + Ararat — mornings only, hazy by afternoon. Martvili Canyon — after 14:00. Batumi Ali & Nino — dusk. Noravank red canyon — 17:00–19:00 July sunset. Lake Sevan — sunrise.

🎥 Content that performs: "Countries most people don't know exist" · "Cheapest beautiful trip in Europe" · "Georgian food I'd never heard of" · "Haunting abandoned USSR spa" · "I found the real Game of Thrones"

spots.description: The most viral spot in Georgia — 14th-century stone church at 2,170m with Mt. Kazbek (5,047m) rising behind it. Every major travel influencer has posted from here. Drone footage regularly hits millions of views. Best shot: sunrise mist (05:30–06:30) before clouds form — only possible if you overnight in Stepantsminda.

image.alt: Gergeti Trinity Church on a green ridge with Mt. Kazbek's snowy peak behind it

spots.title: Gergeti Trinity Church

spots.description: Turquoise emerald water in a dramatic gorge — looks almost CGI in photos. The slow boat ride is one of the most replicated travel shots from Georgia. Constant "hidden gems" TikTok content. Go after 14:00 to avoid tour groups and get unobstructed shots.

image.alt: Turquoise river running through the forested Martvili Canyon

spots.title: Martvili Canyon

spots.description: The Orbeliani Bath House (blue-gold mosaic facade) is the most photographed building in Tbilisi. The carved wooden balconies, Fabrika hostel courtyard, and Narikala rooftop views drive heavy social content. Tbilisi's nightlife (Bassiani, White Noise) also trends massively in the electronic music community.

image.alt: The blue-tiled Persian-style facade of the Orbeliani Baths in Tbilisi

spots.title: Tbilisi Old Town + Orbeliani Baths

spots.description: Batumi's mix of Soviet summer resort, Dubai-style skyscrapers, and Black Sea palm boulevard is perfect "you won't believe this exists" TikTok content. The Ali & Nino kinetic sculpture (two figures that merge and separate) is endlessly photographed. Adjarian khachapuri food videos perform extremely well too.

image.alt: The Ali and Nino kinetic statue on the Batumi seaside boulevard

spots.title: Batumi Boulevard + Ali & Nino

spots.description: The walled wine town has become Georgia's "romantic getaway" content niche. Drone footage of the ancient walls against vineyards and the Caucasus mountains performs extremely well. The 24/7 wedding palace adds a quirky hook for travel content.

image.alt: Walled hilltop wine town of Sighnaghi with the Caucasus mountains in the distance

spots.title: Sighnaghi — City of Love

spots.description: Increasingly viral with "cave city you've never heard of" framing. 12th-century, 13 storeys, 3,000 cave rooms. Drone footage from above the cliff is particularly striking. Performs well in both travel and history niches — a genuinely extraordinary place.

image.alt: Honeycomb cave dwellings of the Vardzia cave monastery carved into a cliff face

spots.title: Vardzia Cave City

spots.description: Abandoned Soviet spa palaces — crumbling grandeur, overgrown ballrooms, faded murals. The "dark tourism / urbex" niche is exploding on TikTok and Instagram. Sanatorium Medea and Imereti are still accessible. Eerie and beautiful, and totally off the main tourist trail. Easy to add as a half-day from Kutaisi.

image.alt: Abandoned colonnaded Soviet-era Sanatorium of the Ministry of Defence in Tskaltubo

spots.title: Tskaltubo Soviet Sanatoria

spots.description: 6th-century monastery on a clifftop above the confluence of two rivers — one of the most cinematic landscapes in the Caucasus. Frequently described as "Game of Thrones-style" by travel creators. Only 30 min from Tbilisi by marshrutka, making it easy content for anyone based in the capital.

image.alt: The 6th-century Jvari Monastery perched on a cliff above Mtskheta

spots.title: Jvari Monastery + Mtskheta

spots.description: The most iconic image in Armenia — the monastery with Mt. Ararat (5,137m, now in Turkey) looming behind it. Looks almost like a painting. Constantly floods Instagram and TikTok. Go early morning for the clearest skies and sharpest Ararat — the mountain often disappears in afternoon haze.

image.alt: Khor Virap monastery on the Armenian plain with snow-capped Mt. Ararat behind

spots.title: Khor Virap + Mt. Ararat

spots.description: Geghard (UNESCO monastery carved into rock) combined with the Symphony of Stones (natural hexagonal basalt columns) is a double viral hit. The geological formations look almost extraterrestrial. The dark atmospheric interior of Geghard drives "you won't believe this is real" reaction content.

image.alt: The medieval rock-cut Geghard Monastery set in Armenia's Garni gorge

spots.title: Geghard + Symphony of Stones

spots.description: Armenia's most viral urban content. The 572-step white limestone staircase with Mt. Ararat visible from the top at sunset is one of the most replicated shots in Armenian travel photography. Free outdoor sculptures along the steps add extra visual interest.

image.alt: The white limestone staircase of the Yerevan Cascade rising up the hillside

spots.title: The Cascade, Yerevan

spots.description: Armenia's "Blue Eye" — Sevanavank monastery on a peninsula reflected in the vast alpine lake. Travel influencers from the Middle East and Russia have made this increasingly prominent on social media in 2024–25. Best photographed at sunrise when the lake is perfectly still.

image.alt: Sevanavank monastery churches on a peninsula overlooking Lake Sevan

spots.title: Lake Sevan at sunrise

spots.description: Red-rock canyon walls with red-stone medieval churches — a wildly photogenic combination not on the current route but worth adding with a spare day. 2 hrs south of Yerevan. Sunset turns the canyon walls glowing orange — photographers call it Armenia's most beautiful monastery. Hashtag #Noravank has millions of posts.

image.alt: Noravank Monastery's medieval churches against the red-rock cliffs of Vayots Dzor

spots.title: Noravank Monastery

spots.description: Yerevan's pink tuff-stone Republic Square comes alive at night with a synchronised musical fountain show. The warm glow of Armenian stone, dancing water, and festive atmosphere make for beautiful evening content. Free to watch — just show up after dark.

image.alt: Floodlit pink-tuff government buildings of Republic Square in Yerevan at night

spots.title: Republic Square fountains at night

restaurants.callout: Curated eats across the Caucasus — top-rated Georgian & Armenian tables, specialty coffee, and natural-wine bars, plus spots blowing up on TikTok and Instagram. Sorted by rating and review volume.

places.name: Barbarestan
places.description: Dishes revived from a 19th-century cookbook — Tbilisi’s most talked-about table.

places.name: Shavi Lomi (Black Lion)
places.description: Modern Georgian cooking in a bohemian courtyard hidden off the main streets.

places.name: Salobie Bia
places.description: Bright, affordable spot for the classics done well — khinkali, lobio, khachapuri.

places.name: Coffee LAB
places.description: Serious specialty roaster — the go-to for a proper flat white in the capital.

places.name: Fabrika
places.description: Former sewing factory turned buzzing courtyard of bars, cafés and shops.

places.name: 8000 Vintages
places.description: Cellar-deep list of Georgian wines with knowledgeable pours and small plates.

places.name: Palaty
places.description: Atmospheric arts café with an eclectic menu — Kutaisi’s most charming all-day spot.

places.name: Bikentia’s Kebabery
places.description: Legendary one-dish joint: juicy kebabs with raw onion and bread. That’s it.

places.name: Toma’s Wine Cellar
places.description: Family-run cellar serving hearty home cooking and their own qvevri wine.

places.name: Fanfan
places.description: Stylish modern-Georgian kitchen a short walk from the Black Sea boulevard.

places.name: Café Retro
places.description: Reliable all-day café in the centre — big menu, good cakes, fast service.

places.name: Take Five
places.description: Smart little cocktail bar for a nightcap off the seafront.

places.name: 5047 Cafe
places.description: Named after Kazbek’s height — warm café with knockout views toward Gergeti church.

places.name: Rooms Hotel Restaurant
places.description: Floor-to-ceiling mountain views with polished mountain comfort food.

places.name: Pheasant’s Tears
places.description: Pioneering natural-wine winery and restaurant — the reason many detour to Kakheti.

places.name: Okro’s Wines
places.description: Terrace wine bar with Alazani valley views and excellent qvevri tastings.

places.name: Lavash
places.description: Yerevan’s buzziest restaurant — modern Armenian dishes and a constant queue.

places.name: Sherep
places.description: Sibling of Lavash with an open kitchen making fresh lavash and tolma to order.

places.name: Tavern Yerevan
places.description: Crowd-pleasing traditional tavern — grilled khorovats, dolma and cold starters.

places.name: Lumen Coffee
places.description: Clean, design-led specialty café — the best pour-overs in the city.

places.name: In Vino
places.description: Anchor of buzzy Saryan “wine street” — deep Armenian list and easy plates.

places.name: Kchuch
places.description: Riverside favourite serving bubbling clay-pot stews in Armenia’s “little Switzerland”.

restaurants.note: Georgian dinners run late and supras are generous — come hungry. Pheasant’s Tears (Sighnaghi) and Lavash (Yerevan) book out; reserve ahead. Carry some GEL/AMD for markets and khinkali joints.

budget.totalNote: Japan + Korea are more expensive than Georgia/Armenia — budget accordingly. Flights are the biggest cost driver. Japan is expensive; HK/Taiwan mid-range; Korea reasonable.

flights.note: ⚠️ Booking order: Buy PRG→IST→NRT first (this is the hardest leg to get cheap). Then book HKG→PRG. Then fill in the domestic flights as your dates solidify. For Japan domestic (Okinawa), book as soon as your Tokyo arrival date is confirmed — summer Okinawa flights sell out.

price.note: HK Express very cheap, book ahead

Jiufen lanterns — the Ghibli town of Taiwan

itinerary.callout: 33-day loop: Prague → Istanbul (layover) → Tokyo → Kyoto/Osaka → Hiroshima → Okinawa → Seoul → Busan/DMZ → Taipei → Hong Kong → Macau → Prague. This is the most logical routing — you never backtrack and cover everything in one sweep.

items.description: Metro M11 from airport to Gayrettepe, then M2 toward city. Or airport bus Havaist to Taksim (~50 min, 100 TRY). Leave luggage at airport storage (IST has 24hr lockers) or at a hotel near Sultanahmet.
items.title: Istanbul Airport (IST) → Old City (Sultanahmet)

items.description: One of the world's great buildings — 537 AD Byzantine cathedral turned mosque. The interior is breathtaking. Now an active mosque: remove shoes, cover shoulders/heads. Free entry.
items.title: Hagia Sophia

items.description: 6 minarets, 20,000 İznik tiles inside. Right next to Hagia Sophia. The old Hippodrome plaza connects the two. Atmospheric even from outside.
items.title: Blue Mosque + Hippodrome

items.description: 4,000 shops in the world's oldest covered market. Go to look and soak up the atmosphere — prices are tourist-inflated. Good for spices, Turkish tea, ceramics.
items.title: Grand Bazaar

items.description: The public Şehir Hatları ferry from Eminönü to Karaköy or Üsküdar (~3 TRY) gives you the classic city-and-water view for essentially nothing. Best at golden hour.
items.title: Bosphorus ferry (optional)

items.description: Try simit (sesame bread ring), balık ekmek (fish sandwich by the water), baklava, Turkish tea, or a sit-down lahmacun. Karaköy area has great modern Turkish restaurants.
items.title: Turkish food evening

items.description: Budget hotel in Sultanahmet (within walking distance of everything) or near the airport if your onward flight is very early.
items.title: Overnight: Istanbul — 1 night

days.subtitle: Hagia Sophia · Grand Bazaar · Bosphorus · Turkish food
days.title: Istanbul layover — Old City highlights

items.description: Turkish Airlines flies IST→NRT direct ~12hrs. Arrives Narita (NRT) or Haneda (HND). Narita Express (N'EX) to Shinjuku: ~90 min, ¥3,070. Haneda: Tokyo Monorail ~25 min, ¥500. Buy IC card (Suica/Pasmo) at airport — works on all trains, buses, many vending machines.
items.title: Istanbul IST → Tokyo NRT or HND (direct or 1 stop)

items.description: Start at the highest point for orientation. Tokyo Skytree (634m) at Asakusa district. Book observation deck in advance in peak season.
items.title: Tokyo Tower or Skytree (Day 2)

items.description: Tokyo's oldest temple, dating to 628 AD. The Nakamise shopping street leading to it is atmospheric. Best in the morning before crowds arrive.
items.title: Asakusa & Senso-ji Temple

items.description: Anime, manga, electronics, gaming mecca. Multi-floor Yodobashi camera + retro game shops + maid cafes. Chaotic and uniquely Tokyo.
items.title: Akihabara — Electric Town

items.description: The world's busiest pedestrian crossing (Shibuya) is most impressive at evening rush hour. Then Shinjuku Golden Gai — 200+ tiny bars in a maze of alleyways. Very photogenic.
items.title: Shibuya Crossing + Shinjuku Golden Gai

items.description: The most viral attraction in all of Japan — immersive digital art installations. Book weeks in advance. teamLab Planets in Toyosu is slightly cheaper and equally stunning.
items.title: teamLab Borderless or Planets

items.description: Shinjuku or Shibuya area for best transport links. Capsule hotel if budget, or business hotel (APA, Dormy Inn etc).
items.title: Overnight: Tokyo — 3 nights

days.subtitle: Shinjuku · Shibuya · Akihabara · Asakusa · teamLab
days.title: Tokyo — Arrival & City Immersion

items.description: Nozomi/Hikari from Tokyo Station. ~2h 15min. ¥13,850 (~€85). Covered by JR Pass if you have one — consider if doing Japan extensively. Book seat online at jrailpass.com or at station.
items.title: Tokyo → Kyoto (Shinkansen bullet train)

items.description: 10,000 red torii gates winding up a mountain — Japan's most viral Instagram spot. Go at 5–6am before the crowds. The hike to the summit takes 2–3 hrs through complete forests of gates. Free and open 24/7.
items.title: Fushimi Inari Shrine (5am visit!)

items.description: The iconic bamboo forest walk (best at dawn). Tenryu-ji Zen garden with a raked gravel garden and pond is stunning. Combine with a boat ride on the Hozu River.
items.title: Arashiyama Bamboo Grove + Tenryu-ji

items.description: Gion is Kyoto's geisha district — best early morning or evening for geisha spotting. Golden Pavilion (Kinkaku-ji) is essential but crowded. Arrive when it opens at 9am.
items.title: Gion District + Kinkaku-ji

items.description: 1,200 free-roaming deer bow for deer crackers and wander the ancient Todai-ji temple grounds. The giant Buddha inside Todai-ji is one of Japan's great sights. Very Instagrammable.
items.title: Nara day trip (30 min from Kyoto)

items.description: Traditional machiya guesthouse (highly recommended) or business hotel near Kyoto Station.
items.title: Overnight: Kyoto — 3 nights

days.subtitle: Fushimi Inari · Arashiyama · Gion · Geisha district · Nara day trip
days.title: Kyoto — Ancient Capital

items.description: JR Biwako Line direct: 15–20 min, ¥570. Or stay in Osaka (slightly cheaper, great food base).
items.title: Kyoto → Osaka (15 min by JR, or Hankyu 30 min)

items.description: 16th-century castle with a great city view from the top. Museum inside is informative. Lovely park surroundings — perfect for morning walk.
items.title: Osaka Castle

items.description: The most photogenic street in Japan — giant glowing Glico Man, crab signs, neon everywhere. Best at night. Eat: takoyaki (octopus balls), okonomiyaki (savory pancake), kushi-katsu (fried skewers). Osaka is Japan's food capital.
items.title: Dotonbori — Food street heaven

items.description: Kuromon is Osaka's bustling covered food market — fresh seafood, wagyu, fruits. Shinsekai is a retro 1950s neighbourhood with cheap kushikatsu restaurants.
items.title: Kuromon Market + Shinsekai

days.subtitle: Dotonbori · Osaka Castle · Takoyaki · Okonomiyaki
days.title: Osaka — Food Capital

items.description: Nozomi from Shin-Osaka: ~45 min, ¥10,440. Or Hikari (slower, JR Pass compatible): ~1h 20min. Day trip from Osaka possible, or stay overnight in Hiroshima.
items.title: Osaka → Hiroshima (Shinkansen, ~1hr 20min)

items.description: One of the most moving places in the world. The A-Bomb Dome (skeletal building that survived the blast), eternal flame, and Children's Peace Monument. The museum is essential — graphic but necessary. Allow 2–3 hrs.
items.title: Hiroshima Peace Memorial Park + Museum

items.description: The "floating" red torii gate in the sea is one of Japan's three most iconic views. Ferry from Hiroshima port (25 min). At high tide the gate appears to float. Sacred deer wander freely. Hike Mt. Misen for panoramic views.
items.title: Miyajima Island — Itsukushima Shrine

items.description: Hiroshima-style okonomiyaki layers ingredients instead of mixing them — noodles, cabbage, pork, egg, bonito. Completely different from Osaka style. The covered Okonomimura building has 27 restaurants on multiple floors.
items.title: Hiroshima Okonomiyaki

days.subtitle: Peace Memorial · Atomic Bomb Dome · Floating Torii Gate
days.title: Hiroshima + Miyajima Island

items.description: Peach, Jetstar, or ANA. ~1h 30min from Osaka. Book well ahead — Okinawa is Japan's most popular domestic beach destination. From ¥4,000 one-way with budget airlines.
items.title: Osaka KIX or Hiroshima HIJ → Okinawa OKA (budget flight)

items.description: Okinawa has the best beaches in Japan — clear turquoise water, white sand, coral. Zanpa Point on the west coast has dramatic limestone cliffs meeting the sea. Renting a scooter or car is essential for beach-hopping (no public bus to many beaches).
items.title: Emerald Beach + Zanpa Beach

items.description: Okinawa has some of Asia's best diving — coral reefs, manta rays, sea turtles. Many operators offer introductory dives for non-certified divers. Kabira Bay on Ishigaki (45 min flight) is legendary.
items.title: Scuba or snorkelling

items.description: Seat of the ancient Ryukyu Kingdom — rebuilt after WWII, partially burned in 2019, still being restored. Distinct from mainland Japanese castles — red lacquer, Chinese influences. Fascinating history.
items.title: Shuri Castle (UNESCO)

items.description: One of the world's great aquariums — a 7.5m deep tank with whale sharks and manta rays. Very photogenic. Allow 2 hrs. In the Ocean Expo Park, also free outdoor areas.
items.title: Churaumi Aquarium

items.description: Naha city (central) or beachside resort in Chatan. Naha has better public transport; Chatan is better for beach access.
items.title: Overnight: Okinawa — 3 nights

days.subtitle: Beaches · Shuri Castle · Churaumi Aquarium · Scuba
days.title: Okinawa — Tropical Japan

items.description: Korean Air, Jeju Air, Jin Air. ~2 hrs. From Seoul Incheon Airport: AREX Express train to Seoul Station 43 min, ₩9,500 (~€7). Or suburban train ₩4,150 (~€3), ~50 min. Buy T-money card for all Seoul public transport (metro + bus + some taxis).
items.title: Okinawa OKA → Seoul ICN (direct, ~2hrs)

items.description: The grandest of Seoul's Five Grand Palaces. Changing of the Guard ceremony at 10am and 2pm daily. Rent hanbok at the gate for free/discounted entry and beautiful photos.
items.title: Gyeongbokgung Palace + Changing of the Guard

items.description: Traditional Korean wooden houses (hanok) on a hillside with views of the modern city behind. Very photogenic — one of Seoul's most Instagrammed spots. Go early morning before the tour groups arrive.
items.title: Bukchon Hanok Village

items.description: Seoul's most famous shopping street — K-beauty products at every corner, street food, cosmetics. Namdaemun is Korea's largest traditional market — great for clothes and food at 2am.
items.title: Myeongdong + Namdaemun Market

items.description: Hongdae is student nightlife (indie, electronic, live music). Itaewon is the international district — more diverse bars and clubs. Both are excellent.
items.title: Hongdae + Itaewon nightlife

items.description: Cable car up Namsan Hill, then the N Tower for 360° views of Seoul. The "love locks" on the fence and the coloured LED lighting at night are very photogenic.
items.title: N Seoul Tower + Namsan Hill

items.description: Hongdae, Myeongdong, or Jongno area for best location. Many excellent capsule hotels in Seoul.
items.title: Overnight: Seoul — 4 nights (3 here + 1 after DMZ)

days.subtitle: Gyeongbokgung · Bukchon · Myeongdong · Hongdae nightlife
days.title: Seoul — Dynamic Capital

items.description: Several tour operators: USO tours, Korail DMZ train, or private companies (Klook, GetYourGuide). Most include JSA (Joint Security Area/Panmunjom) — the only place you can stand on the North Korean side. Book 2–3 weeks ahead — JSA often sells out. Departs ~08:00, returns ~18:00.
items.title: Seoul → DMZ (guided tour bus, departs early morning)

items.description: The only place where you can stand on North Korean soil. Blue UN buildings straddling the actual border. UN soldiers stand at attention facing North Korea. Utterly surreal. Strict dress code — no ripped clothes, no sandals.
items.title: Joint Security Area (JSA) / Panmunjom

items.description: A tunnel dug by North Korea discovered in 1978 — designed to move 30,000 troops per hour under the border. You walk down 365m into the tunnel (hard hats provided). Claustrophobic and fascinating.
items.title: Third Infiltration Tunnel

items.description: The observatory has binoculars facing into North Korea — on clear days you can see Kaesong and the propaganda village of Kijong-dong. Dorasan Station is a train station with no trains — Seoul's northernmost station, ready and waiting for reunification.
items.title: Dora Observatory + Dorasan Station

days.subtitle: Joint Security Area · Third Tunnel · Panmunjom
days.title: DMZ — North Korean Border

items.description: KTX from Seoul Station. ~2h 30min, ₩59,800 (~€43). Several per hour. Book at letskorail.com or Korail app. One of the world's great train rides.
items.title: Seoul → Busan (KTX bullet train, ~2h 30min)

items.description: Korea's most famous beach — 1.8km of fine sand, very crowded in July/August but atmospheric. The Haeundae district has great restaurants and nightlife.
items.title: Haeundae Beach

items.description: The "Machu Picchu of Busan" — a hillside neighbourhood of brightly painted houses in pastel shades, with art installations and murals weaving through the narrow alleys. One of Korea's most Instagrammed spots.
items.title: Gamcheon Culture Village

items.description: A rare coastal Buddhist temple built on cliffs above the East Sea — exceptionally beautiful and photogenic. Very different from inland Korean temples.
items.title: Haedong Yonggungsa Temple

items.description: Korea's largest seafood market — raw fish, live tanks, second-floor restaurants where you take your purchase to be cooked. The chaos and energy is extraordinary.
items.title: Jagalchi Fish Market

items.description: Haeundae or Gwangalli Beach area for beach access, or Seomyeon for central nightlife.
items.title: Overnight: Busan — 3 nights

days.subtitle: Haeundae Beach · Gamcheon Village · Haedong Temple
days.title: Busan — Korea's Beach City

items.description: EVA Air, China Airlines, Korean Air. ~2–2.5 hrs. MRT to central Taipei: Taoyuan MRT to Taipei Main Station, ~35 min, NT$150 (~€4.50). Buy EasyCard (IC card) for all MRT and buses.
items.title: Busan PUS or Seoul ICN → Taipei TPE (direct, ~2–2.5hrs)

items.description: Once the world's tallest building — 508m. Fast elevator to 89F observation deck. Best at dusk when you see the city light up.
items.title: Taipei 101 observation deck

items.description: Taipei's largest and most famous night market. Stinky tofu, scallion pancakes, oyster vermicelli, bubble tea, roasted corn. The "giant fried chicken" here is Instagram-famous. Go hungry at 19:00.
items.title: Shilin Night Market (evening)

items.description: Mountainside old town with red lanterns, tea houses, steep stone steps, and sea views — said to have inspired Studio Ghibli's "Spirited Away." One of Asia's most photographed places. Bus from Taipei ~1.5 hrs.
items.title: Jiufen — Gold Rush hilltown (day trip)

items.description: North coast sea-eroded sandstone formations — the "Queen's Head" rock is the most photographed. Dramatic alien landscape. Bus from Taipei ~1 hr.
items.title: Yehliu Geopark (day trip)

items.description: Natural sulphur hot spring resort district accessible by MRT (~30 min). Public baths from NT$80, private pools from NT$400/hr. Very popular with locals after hiking.
items.title: Beitou Hot Springs

items.description: Da'an or Zhongshan district for best access. Ximending (youth area) for budget options.
items.title: Overnight: Taipei — 4 nights

days.subtitle: Taipei 101 · Jiufen · Yehliu · Night markets · Hot springs
days.title: Taipei — Night Markets & Mountains

items.description: Cathay Pacific, EVA Air, HK Express. ~1.5 hrs. Airport Express to Hong Kong Station: 24 min, HK$115 (~€14). Buy Octopus card for all HK public transport (MTR, bus, tram, Star Ferry).
items.title: Taipei TPE → Hong Kong HKG (direct, ~1.5hrs)

items.description: The iconic view of Hong Kong's skyline from above — one of the world's great city panoramas. Take the Peak Tram (HK$72 return) or hike the Governor's Walk (free). Go at sunset into nighttime for the double effect.
items.title: Victoria Peak (The Peak)

items.description: The legendary 7-min ferry between Kowloon and Hong Kong Island — HK$3.40. The best value activity in Asia. At night the LED skyline light show is stunning. Run since 1888.
items.tag: HK$3.40 (~€0.40) — one of the world's great cheap experiences
items.title: Star Ferry crossing

items.description: Mongkok has some of the world's highest population density — dense electronics shops, women's street, flower market, bird market. Temple Street Night Market: fortune tellers, Cantonese opera, seafood restaurants.
items.title: Mongkok + Temple Street Night Market

items.description: HK$2.60 for a ride on Hong Kong Island's historic double-decker trams — go to the upper deck front seat. One of the world's great cheap city experiences.
items.title: Hong Kong Tram (Ding Ding) ride

items.description: The hike behind Kowloon to Lion Rock (500m) gives a top-down view of the city that no observation deck can match. 2–3 hours. Completely free.
items.title: Lion Rock / Kowloon hike

items.description: Mong Kok (Kowloon side) for budget, or Wan Chai/Causeway Bay (HK Island) for mid-range.
items.title: Overnight: Hong Kong — 3 nights

days.subtitle: Victoria Peak · Star Ferry · Night skyline · Mongkok
days.title: Hong Kong — Vertical City

items.description: From Sheung Wan Macau Ferry Terminal or Tsim Sha Tsui China Ferry Terminal. Journey ~1 hr. Tickets HK$180–220. Departs every 15–30 min throughout the day. Passport required! Return ferries run until midnight.
items.title: Hong Kong → Macau (TurboJet or Cotai Jet ferry)

items.description: The baroque stone facade of a 17th-century cathedral — one of Asia's most iconic ruins. The cobblestone Senado Square (Portuguese wave-pattern paving) in front is extremely photogenic.
items.title: Ruins of St Paul's + Senado Square (UNESCO)

items.description: Macau's culinary legacy from 400 years of Portuguese rule. Lord Stow's Bakery in Coloane (the original) or Margaret's Café e Nata. Best eaten warm, immediately out of the oven.
items.title: Portuguese egg tarts (pastel de nata)

items.description: You don't need to gamble to enjoy the casino strip — the free shows, architecture, and sheer excess are surreal entertainment. The Venetian's indoor canal with gondoliers is peak kitsch.
items.title: Casino strip walk — The Venetian, Parisian, Grand Lisboa

items.description: The oldest lighthouse on the China coast (1865), set in a forested hill above the city. Free. Good views and a lovely escape from the casino madness.
items.title: Guia Fortress + lighthouse

days.subtitle: Historic Old City · Ruins of St Paul · Casino strip · Ferry back
days.title: Macau day trip — Las Vegas of Asia

items.description: Cathay Pacific via London, or Emirates via Dubai, or Turkish Airlines via Istanbul. ~13–16 hrs total. Book as part of your return ticket. Compare at Google Flights: Cathay CX → oneworld partners, or Turkish TK for best Europe connections.
items.title: Hong Kong HKG → Prague PRG (via hub)

items.description: The giant Tian Tan Buddha statue (34m) on a mountain top reached by the Ngong Ping 360 cable car (very photogenic). The cable car journey over sea and mountains is stunning. Po Lin Monastery is right next to it.
items.title: Optional: Lantau Island + Big Buddha

items.description: Last chance for electronics (Sham Shui Po), street markets, or Harbour City mall. Hong Kong has zero VAT — good for duty-free goods.
items.title: Last shopping: Mong Kok or Times Square

days.subtitle: Buffer days · HKG → Prague (via hub) · Extended time if needed
days.title: Return home + Optional extra days

subtitle: Istanbul layover · Tokyo · Kyoto · Osaka · Hiroshima · Okinawa · Seoul · Busan · DMZ · Taipei · Hong Kong · Macau
tagline: 33-day sweep across six countries — from a Istanbul layover through Japan, Korea, Taiwan, Hong Kong, and Macau.

cards.body: Japan is still largely cash-heavy — carry ¥5,000–10,000 daily. 7-Eleven and Japan Post ATMs accept foreign cards (most do). Korea: widely card-friendly. HK/Taiwan: mix of cash and card. Use Revolut or Wise for fee-free withdrawals everywhere.
cards.title: Money & Cards

cards.body: Get these at the airport — they'll save you endless hassle. Japan: Suica (works Japan-wide). Korea: T-money. Taiwan: EasyCard. Hong Kong: Octopus. All work tap-in/out on trains, buses, and many shops.
cards.title: IC Transit Cards

cards.body: Japan: rent a pocket WiFi at Narita/Haneda airport (¥300–500/day) or buy a data SIM (IIJmio, Mobal). Korea/Taiwan/HK: buy a local data SIM at the airport — much cheaper. Google Fi works in all these countries if you have it.
cards.title: Pocket WiFi / SIM

cards.body: Must be bought outside Japan. Buy at jrailpass.com before departure — it's physically cheaper and activates on your start date in Japan. 7-day pass covers Tokyo↔Hiroshima route perfectly. Nozomi bullet trains are NOT included.
cards.title: JR Pass — Buy Abroad

cards.body: Czech passport: no visa needed in any of these countries (Japan 90 days, Korea 90 days, Taiwan 90 days, HK 90 days, Macau 90 days, Turkey 90 days). Just show up. Keep passport valid 6 months beyond your return date.
cards.title: Visas

cards.body: Japan has expensive healthcare — always buy travel insurance for this trip. Cover should include medical evacuation. European Health Card (EHIC) does NOT cover Japan/Korea. ARAG, AXA, or World Nomads are good options for European travellers.
cards.title: Travel Insurance

cards.body: Remove shoes at temples and many restaurants (look for a step up and shoe rack). Don't eat or drink while walking. No tipping — ever — in Japan or Korea. Quietness on public transport is expected (headphones always, phone calls at a whisper). Both hands when giving/receiving things.
cards.title: Japan Etiquette

cards.body: July–August is hot and humid across all these countries (35°C+ in Tokyo/Seoul/Taipei). Pack moisture-wicking clothes, carry a foldable fan, and drink constantly. Okinawa has typhoon season June–September — check forecasts. Mountain areas (Arashiyama, Jeju) are cooler.
cards.title: Summer Heat

cards.body: Japan's most popular attractions need advance booking: teamLab, Ghibli Museum (lottery, must book 2 months ahead!), some ryokan (traditional inns). JSA/DMZ tour: book 2–3 weeks ahead. Okinawa flights: 6–8 weeks ahead. Everything else is walkup.
cards.title: Booking in advance

cards.body: Japan: convenience store (konbini) food is shockingly good and cheap — 7-Eleven onigiri for ¥140. Ramen, sushi conveyor belts, gyudon chains (Yoshinoya). Korea: Korean BBQ, bibimbap, tteokbokki, fried chicken with beer ("chimaek"). Taiwan: night market food is the whole point. HK: dim sum morning yum cha.
cards.title: Food Tips

tips.note:
⏱️ Timing note: 33 days is ambitious but doable. If you need to cut, the easiest trimming is: drop Okinawa (it adds 2 flights and is less culturally rich than the mainland cities), or shorten Busan to 2 nights instead of 3. Japan needs the most time — don't cut Japan short.

📞 Emergency contacts: Japan police 110, ambulance 119. Korea 119 (emergency). Taiwan 119. Hong Kong 999. Turkey 155 (police), 112 (emergency).

title: East Asia

transport.callout: Japan and Korea have world-class public transport. Get a Suica/Pasmo card (Japan) and T-money card (Korea) from airport arrivals — they work on nearly everything. Hong Kong's Octopus card is equally essential. Taiwan's EasyCard covers all MRT and most buses.

chips.label: Traffic can be terrible — avoid taxis at rush hour

steps.text: Take Metro M11 from IST to Gayrettepe (~30 min). Transfer to M2 toward Yenikapı. Get off at Vezneciler or Fatih for Sultanahmet.
Or Havaist airport bus to Taksim Square (~50 min, 100 TRY). Then taxi or metro to Old City.
Within Old City: walk (10–15 min between Hagia Sophia, Blue Mosque, Grand Bazaar). Or trams T1 line.

chips.label: Top up at any ticket machine

steps.text: Get a Suica or Pasmo card from any JR ticket machine at the airport. Load with ¥3,000–5,000 to start. Add more at any station.
Touch in/out at every fare gate. Works on JR trains, Tokyo/Osaka metro, most buses, and increasingly at convenience stores and vending machines.

routes.subtitle: Works on all JR, subway, bus, and taxis in major cities
routes.title: IC Card (Suica / Pasmo) — get this first!

chips.label: Nozomi NOT included in JR Pass

steps.text: From Tokyo Station, take Nozomi (fastest, 2h 15min) or Hikari (JR Pass compatible, ~2h 45min) to Kyoto or Shin-Osaka.
Reserve a seat online (smartex.jr-central.co.jp) or buy at the "Green Window" ticket counter. Non-reserved cars available for spontaneous travel.

chips.label: Miyajima ferry included in JR Pass

steps.text: From Shin-Osaka, Nozomi to Hiroshima ~45 min. Or Hikari (JR Pass): ~1h 20min.
From Hiroshima Station, take Hiroshima city tram (#2 or #6) to Peace Park: ~20 min, ¥180. Or Sanyo Line to Miyajima-guchi then ferry.

chips.label: Book Okinawa flights early — summer fills up
Peach often under ¥5,000 with advance booking

steps.text: Budget airlines: Peach (from KIX) or Jetstar Japan (from KIX/HND). Book 6–8 weeks ahead for lowest fares — Okinawa is very popular in summer.
From Naha Airport: Yui Rail monorail to central Naha (Kokusai-dori area): 13 min, ¥270. For beaches further north: rent a scooter or car — buses are infrequent.

chips.label: Buy JR Pass BEFORE arriving in Japan (overseas price lower)

steps.text: 7-day JR Pass (¥50,000): only worth it if you do Tokyo→Kyoto→Hiroshima + Miyajima ferry within 7 days. Calculate your planned Shinkansen routes at hyperdia.com first.
14-day JR Pass (¥80,000): usually worth it for Tokyo + Kyoto/Osaka + Hiroshima + Kyushu or Hokkaido. For this itinerary (with Okinawa by budget flight), a 7-day pass covers Tokyo↔Hiroshima perfectly.

routes.subtitle: Only worth it for heavy Shinkansen use
routes.title: Should I buy a JR Pass?

chips.label: T-money works on all Seoul metro, bus, and some taxis

steps.text: Get a T-money card from the airport convenience store. Loads of ₩10,000 to start.
AREX Express to Seoul Station: 43 min, ₩9,500 (~€7). Or AREX All-Stop to any Seoul metro station: ~50–60 min, ₩4,150 (~€3). T-money works on both.
Book a JSA + DMZ combined tour at least 2–3 weeks ahead — JSA visits fill up fast. Operators: USO Tours, Korail DMZ train, or through Klook/GetYourGuide.
Dress code for JSA: no shorts, ripped clothes, or sandals. Military escort inside JSA. No photos except in designated spots.

chips.label: One of the world's great train rides

steps.text: KTX from Seoul Station to Busan Station. ~2h 30min. Book at letskorail.com or Korail app — seats can sell out on weekends and holidays.
From Busan Station: Metro Line 1 to Haeundae beach (10 stops, ~20 min, ₩1,400). Gamcheon Village: Metro to Toseong, then Bus 2 (10 min).

chips.label: MRT is clean, cheap, and easy to use

steps.text: Get an EasyCard from airport convenience store or machine. Works on all Taipei MRT, buses, and YouBike bike share.
Taoyuan MRT to Taipei Main Station: ~35 min. Express (limited stops): NT$150. All-stop: NT$130. Direct into the city centre — easy.

chips.label: Combine Jiufen + Yehliu in one day
Jiufen gets crowded in the afternoon — go morning or evening

steps.text: Jiufen: MRT to Zhongxiao Fuxing, then Bus 1062. Journey ~1.5 hrs, NT$110. Last bus back ~22:30 (check in winter). Or join a cheap tour from Taipei.
Yehliu: Bus from Zhongshan N. Rd Sec. 6 (near MRT Jiantan), Bus 1815. ~1 hr, NT$90. Can combine Jiufen + Yehliu in one day by taxi between the two (~NT$600).

chips.label: AEL in-town check-in is excellent — skip airport queues
Octopus card is essential — get it first

steps.text: Get an Octopus card immediately (HK$150 incl. HK$100 stored value). Works on MTR, all buses, trams, Star Ferry, minibuses, and most 7-Elevens.
Airport Express (AEL) to Hong Kong Station: 24 min, HK$115. Or slower MTR: ~50 min, HK$45. AEL offers in-town check-in at Hong Kong/Kowloon stations — check bags 1 day before your flight!

routes.subtitle: Day 24
routes.title: Hong Kong Airport (HKG) → city + Octopus card

chips.label: Passport required — it's an immigration crossing
Casino shuttles are free and go everywhere

steps.text: From Sheung Wan Macau Ferry Terminal (HK Island) or China Ferry Terminal, Tsim Sha Tsui (Kowloon). TurboJet or Cotai Jet. ~1 hr. Departs every 15–30 min.

Tickets: HK$180–220. Book ahead online or at the terminal. Bring your passport — it's an immigration crossing. Return ferries run until midnight (hourly). Casinos run free shuttles from the ferry terminal.
In Macau: buses MOP$6 for most routes. Free casino shuttle buses go everywhere. Walking is fine for the historic area (Senado Square → St Paul's Ruins → Guia Fortress).

transport.note: Essential apps: Japan: Hyperdia (train routes), Navitime, Google Maps · Korea: Naver Map (better than Google in Korea), KakaoMap, Kakao T (taxis) · Taiwan/HK: Google Maps works great · All: Revolut for fee-free card payments, Grab/Agoda for accommodation.

viral.callout: This route contains more viral travel photography destinations than almost any trip on Earth. Japan alone has dominated travel social media for years. These are the spots generating the most TikTok and Instagram content right now.
viral.note: 🎥 Content formats that work: Japan: "I woke up at 5am and had Fushimi Inari to myself" · "teamLab made me cry" · Korea: "I stood on North Korean soil for €50" · "Korea's secret Machu Picchu" (Gamcheon) · Taiwan: "This town inspired Studio Ghibli" (Jiufen) · Hong Kong: "The world's best view for 40 cents" (Star Ferry)

spots.description: 10,000 red torii gates winding up a forested mountain — Japan's most viral image. The secret is time: go at 5–6am and you'll have the first few hundred gates to yourself. Thousands of TikTok videos daily. Free, open 24/7. The upper sections (30–60 min up) are almost always crowd-free.

image.alt: Tunnel of vermillion torii gates and a hanging stone lantern at Fushimi Inari-taisha, Kyoto

spots.title: Fushimi Inari at Dawn

spots.description: Immersive digital art installations — walking into infinity mirror rooms, fields of floating lights, and environments that react to your movement. The single most shared experience in Tokyo on social media. Book weeks ahead. Borderless has relocated to Azabudai Hills — check current location.

image.alt: Visitors immersed in a Light Sculpture installation at teamLab Borderless, Tokyo

spots.title: teamLab Borderless / Planets

spots.description: 1,200 sacred deer that actually bow to ask for deer crackers. The deer-bowing interaction is one of Japan's most shared animal encounters on TikTok. They can be surprisingly assertive and will headbutt you for crackers — which makes for incredible comedic content.

image.alt: Sika deer grazing among visitors in Nara Park, Japan

spots.title: Nara Deer Bowing

spots.description: The "floating" red torii gate at high tide is one of Japan's three most iconic views. At low tide you can walk to it, which is also viral — oysters growing on the base. Evening golden light with the gate silhouetted against the sea performs extremely well on all platforms.

image.alt: The vermillion torii of Itsukushima Shrine appearing to float on the sea, Miyajima

spots.title: Miyajima Floating Torii Gate

spots.description: The world's busiest pedestrian crossing — 3,000 people cross simultaneously at rush hour. Best photographed from the Starbucks or Mag's Park above. At night, the neon reflections in the wet crossing surface (rain) create one of urban photography's most replicated images.

image.alt: Aerial view of the scramble crossing at Shibuya, Tokyo

spots.title: Shibuya Crossing at night

spots.description: Soaring bamboo stalks creating a cathedral-like green tunnel — Japan's most tranquil viral spot. The sound of the wind through the bamboo is unique. Dawn (before 6am) or sunset gives empty paths. At midday in summer it's packed with tour groups.

image.alt: Sunlight filtering through towering bamboo stalks in the Arashiyama Bamboo Grove, Kyoto

spots.title: Arashiyama Bamboo Grove

spots.description: Stacked pastel houses cascading down a hillside with art installations winding through narrow alleys — called the "Machu Picchu of Busan." Every Instagram wall has a different mural. The contrast of vivid colours against the grey Busan industrial backdrop makes for extraordinary photography.

image.alt: Pastel-colored houses cascading down the hillside of Gamcheon Culture Village, Busan

spots.title: Gamcheon Culture Village

spots.description: Traditional Korean wooden houses with tiled roofs on a hillside with the Seoul skyscrapers behind — an extraordinary visual contrast. Go at 6–7am to have the alleys to yourself. Residents have signs asking for quiet. The misty morning views down the alleys are extremely shareable.

image.alt: Traditional tiled-roof hanok houses lining a narrow lane in Bukchon, Seoul

spots.title: Bukchon Hanok Village at dawn

spots.description: Standing in the blue UN buildings straddling the world's most heavily fortified border — with North Korean soldiers visible metres away — generates some of travel photography's most emotionally resonant content. "I stood on North Korean soil" content performs extremely well.

image.alt: Blue JSA buildings at Panmunjom with Panmungak Hall behind in North Korea

spots.title: DMZ / North Korean Border

spots.description: A Buddhist temple built directly on dramatic sea cliffs — utterly unique in Korea. Pagodas and statues with the churning East Sea below. Dawn photos here with the rising sun and mist are among Korea's most shared travel images.

image.alt: Haedong Yonggungsa Buddhist temple perched on the coastal cliffs near Busan

spots.title: Haedong Yonggungsa Temple

spots.description: Red lanterns glowing over stone steps descending into sea fog — said to have inspired the Spirit World scenes in Studio Ghibli's "Spirited Away." Evening is when it truly comes alive. The iconic shot from the tea house stairs looking down the lantern-lit steps is one of Asia's most viral images.

image.alt: Red lanterns glowing over the stone-stepped streets of Jiufen at night, Taiwan

spots.title: Jiufen Old Street at night

spots.description: Taiwan's night markets generate more food TikTok content per square metre than almost anywhere on Earth. Giant popcorn chicken, stinky tofu steam clouds, bubble tea being made, grilled corn — all highly shareable. The smells and chaos are unique. Best content window: 19:00–21:00.

image.alt: Crowded food alley packed with stalls and signage at Shilin Night Market, Taipei

spots.title: Shilin Night Market food content

spots.description: Hong Kong's skyscraper-packed skyline reflected in Victoria Harbour at night is consistently rated one of the world's great urban views. The Symphony of Lights show (20:00 daily, 8 min) is one of the largest permanent light and sound shows in the world. Best viewed from the Tsim Sha Tsui Promenade.

image.alt: Nighttime view of the illuminated Hong Kong Island skyline across Victoria Harbour

spots.title: Victoria Harbour Skyline at night

spots.description: One of the world's great cheap travel experiences — 7 minutes across Victoria Harbour for HK$3.40 (~€0.40). At night the city shimmers behind. Travel content about "the best view money can buy" with this price tag consistently goes viral. The green wooden ferry is iconic in itself.

image.alt: A green-and-white Star Ferry crossing Victoria Harbour, Hong Kong

spots.title: Star Ferry crossing (HK$3.40)

spots.description: The baroque stone facade of a 17th-century Jesuit cathedral standing alone — one of Asia's most recognisable heritage images. The Portuguese cobblestone waves of Senado Square in front create a European-in-Asia visual that performs extremely well for travel content.

image.alt: The ornate baroque stone facade of the Ruins of St. Paul's, Macau

spots.title: Ruins of St Paul's, Macau

spots.description: 5.7km cable car journey over Lantau's mountains and sea to the world's tallest outdoor seated bronze Buddha. The cable car glass-bottom cabin option is terrifying and very shareable. The statue from below at dawn with mist in the valley is one of HK's most powerful images.

image.alt: The seated bronze Tian Tan Big Buddha statue atop Ngong Ping, Lantau Island

spots.title: Ngong Ping Cable Car + Big Buddha

restaurants.callout: A six-country food crawl — ramen counters, night-market stalls, dim sum, and world-ranked cocktail bars, mixing Google’s highest-rated tables with viral TikTok/Instagram finds. Sorted by rating × review volume.

places.name: Çiya Sofrası
places.description: Legendary Kadıköy kitchen reviving rare Anatolian dishes — pay by weight at the counter.

places.name: Hamdi Restaurant
places.description: Eminönü kebab institution with a rooftop view over the Golden Horn.

places.name: Mandabatmaz
places.description: Hidden Beyoğlu nook famous for impossibly thick, rich Turkish coffee.

places.name: Mikla
places.description: Rooftop bar atop the Marmara Pera with a 360° sweep over the old city.

places.name: Sushi Dai
places.description: The pilgrimage sushi counter (now at Toyosu) — go at dawn for the omakase.

places.name: Ichiran Shibuya
places.description: Famous solo-booth tonkotsu ramen chain — the viral “focus” ramen experience.

places.name: Fuglen Tokyo
places.description: Oslo coffee by day, cocktails by night, in a vintage-styled Tomigaya space.

places.name: Bar High Five
places.description: Ginza basement temple of the craft cocktail — tell the master your mood.

places.name: Shinjuku Golden Gai
places.description: Warren of six-seat bars in postwar alleys — bar-hop and soak up the atmosphere.

places.name: Kichi Kichi Omurice
places.description: Chef Yukimura’s theatrical tableside omurice — book well ahead, it’s tiny.

places.name: Nishiki Market
places.description: Five-block covered market — graze skewers, tofu doughnuts, pickles and matcha.

places.name: % Arabica Kyoto Arashiyama
places.description: The riverside flagship with bamboo-grove views — the most Instagrammed coffee in Japan.

places.name: L’Escamoteur Bar
places.description: Theatrical, dimly-lit cocktail den near Shijo — inventive, beautifully made drinks.

places.name: Mizuno Okonomiyaki
places.description: Dotonbori okonomiyaki master with fluffy yamaimo batter — worth the wait.

places.name: Takoyaki Wanaka
places.description: Reliable street-corner takoyaki — molten octopus balls done the Osaka way.

places.name: Lilo Coffee Roasters
places.description: Hip Amerikamura roaster pulling bright, modern espresso for the cool crowd.

places.name: Bar Nayuta
places.description: Tiny, exacting cocktail bar — let the bartender build to your taste.

places.name: Hassei
places.description: Beloved spot for layered Hiroshima-style okonomiyaki — try the oyster version.

places.name: Yakigaki no Hayashi
places.description: Miyajima island favourite for fat grilled and raw oysters near the floating torii.

places.name: Yunangi
places.description: Homely spot for Okinawan home cooking — rafute pork, goya chanpuru and awamori.

places.name: Vongo & Anchor
places.description: Airy seaside café in American Village — strong coffee and all-day brunch.

places.name: Gwangjang Market
places.description: The street-food market — mung-bean pancakes, “drug” gimbap and live octopus.

places.name: Tosokchon Samgyetang
places.description: Famous ginseng-chicken soup near the palace — restorative and always busy.

places.name: Onion Anguk
places.description: Bakery-café set in a traditional hanok — pastries and coffee in courtyard calm.

places.name: Le Chamber
places.description: Library-door speakeasy in Gangnam — a regular on Asia’s best-bars lists.

places.name: Jagalchi Fish Market
places.description: Korea’s largest fish market — choose your catch downstairs, eat it cooked upstairs.

places.name: Ssiat Hotteok
places.description: Busan’s signature seed-stuffed sweet pancake — the BIFF Square street snack.

places.name: Momos Coffee
places.description: Home of a World Barista Champion — arguably Korea’s most celebrated roaster.

places.name: Din Tai Fung (Xinyi)
places.description: The global xiaolongbao icon at its flagship — watch the soup-dumpling assembly line.

places.name: Raohe Street Night Market
places.description: One of Taipei’s best night markets — start with the black-pepper buns at the gate.

places.name: Fika Fika Cafe
places.description: Nordic-leaning café from a champion roaster — light, clean, expertly pulled.

places.name: Indulge Experimental Bistro
places.description: Pioneering cocktail bar in a converted mansion — a fixture on Asia’s best lists.

places.name: Tim Ho Wan
places.description: The famously affordable Michelin dim sum — order the baked BBQ-pork buns.

places.name: Australia Dairy Company
places.description: Frantic Jordan diner famous for pillowy scrambled eggs and steamed milk pudding.

places.name: % Arabica (Star Ferry)
places.description: Harbourfront kiosk by the Star Ferry — coffee with a Victoria Harbour view.

places.name: COA
places.description: Agave-focused bar that has topped Asia’s 50 Best — superb mezcal cocktails.

places.name: Lord Stow’s Bakery
places.description: Coloane village original of the Macau Portuguese egg tart — get them warm.

places.name: Restaurante Litoral
places.description: Benchmark Macanese kitchen for African chicken and minchi — the fusion that defines Macau.

places.name: Tai Lei Loi Kei
places.description: Taipa institution for the crisp Macanese pork-chop bun — grab and go.

restaurants.note: Many top spots (Sushi Dai, Bar High Five, Din Tai Fung) have queues or few seats — go early or off-peak. Night markets are best after 18:00. Carry cash for stalls; cards are fine at sit-down places.

budget.totalNote: All prices are per person. Entrance fees are approximate — verify at official sites before visiting. CZK to EUR ≈ 25:1.
