flights.note: No flights needed — Kutná Hora is a 1-hour direct train ride from Prague. Buy tickets via the Můj vlak (ČD) app or at Praha hlavní nádraží.

highlights: Sedlec Ossuary — 40,000 human bones turned into art
Cathedral of St. Barbara — one of Europe's finest Gothic churches
Italian Court — the original royal mint of Bohemia
Czech Museum of Silver with underground mine tour
Medieval old town streets, stone fountain & plague column
1 hour by direct train from Prague

itinerary.callout: All key sites are walkable once you're in the town center. Sedlec (Ossuary + Cathedral) is ~3 km from the center — take bus #1 or a taxi on arrival, then walk back through town.

items.description: Direct trains run roughly every 2 hours. First convenient departure around 8:00, arriving ~9:05. Buy tickets via the Můj vlak (ČD) app or at the station.
items.title: Train: Praha hl.n. → Kutná Hora hl.n.

items.description: From Kutná Hora hl.n., hop on local bus #1 toward Sedlec — 2 stops, 5 minutes. Gets you to the Ossuary before the tour groups arrive.
items.title: Bus #1 to Sedlec

items.description: The famous bone church decorated with the remains of ~40,000 people. Chandeliers, coats-of-arms, and garlands — all human bone. Budget 30–45 min. Gets crowded after 10am so arriving early is key.
items.title: Sedlec Ossuary (Kostnice)

items.description: A UNESCO-listed Gothic-Baroque cathedral right next door to the Ossuary. Hugely underrated — most tourists skip it. The vast, serene interior is a complete contrast to the Ossuary. 20–30 min.
items.title: Sedlec Cathedral (Cathedral of the Assumption)

items.description: The original royal mint of Bohemia, where Prague Groschen were struck from local silver. Tour the royal apartments, the St. Wenceslas Chapel, and the coin-minting hall. 45–60 min with guided tour.
items.title: Italian Court (Vlašský dvůr)

items.description: Strap on a hard hat and descend into a real 14th-century silver mine under the town. The mine tour takes ~60 min and is genuinely atmospheric. Book ahead in peak season at cms-kutnahora.cz.
items.title: Czech Museum of Silver + Medieval Mine Tour

items.description: One of the most beautiful Gothic churches in Central Europe. The miners' frescoes inside are extraordinary. The terrace approach along the Jesuit College is one of the best walks in the country.
items.title: Cathedral of St. Barbara (Chrám sv. Barbory)

items.description: The long Baroque building flanking the approach to St. Barbara. Walk the full terrace lined with Baroque statues, with views over the Vrchlice valley. One of the best 20-minute walks in Bohemia.
items.title: Jesuit College Promenade (Barborská)

items.description: A beautifully carved Gothic fountain built in 1493, one of the finest surviving Gothic fountains in Bohemia. Easy 10-min stop en route between the cathedral and the town center.
items.title: Stone Fountain (Kamenný Kašna)

items.description: A late-Gothic townhouse with one of the most ornate Gothic facades in the Czech Republic. Worth a close look from outside even if you skip the museum interior. 10 min.
items.title: Stone House (Kamenný Dům)

items.description: Several solid restaurants on the main square. Dobrovická restaurace for hearty Czech food; Café Restaurant Piazza for something lighter. Budget 200–350 CZK for a main + beer.
items.title: Lunch on Palacký Square

items.description: A 14th-century Gothic church right in the town center, notable for its leaning tower. Mix of Gothic and Baroque inside. Free to enter and almost always quiet.
items.title: Church of St. James (Kostel sv. Jakuba)

items.description: The Baroque plague column on the upper square, then a wander down Šultysova — one of the prettiest cobbled streets in the old town. Good for photos and people-watching.
items.title: Plague Column & Šultysova Street

items.description: Czech modern and contemporary art housed inside the former Jesuit College. Even if you skip the exhibitions, the building is spectacular. Allow 45–60 min if you go in.
items.title: GASK — Gallery of the Central Bohemian Region

items.description: Comfortable evening trains back to Prague at roughly 17:00, 19:00, and 21:00. Direct, ~1 hour.
items.title: Train: Kutná Hora hl.n. → Praha hl.n.

days.subtitle: Bones, silver, and Gothic spires
days.title: Kutná Hora Full Day

subtitle: Bones, Gothic Cathedrals & Medieval Silver
tagline: One day to conquer a UNESCO town built on silver and bones.

cards.body: The Ossuary gets very crowded by 10am in peak season. Aim to be there when it opens at 9:00 (Apr–Sep). The first morning train from Prague arrives at ~9:05 — perfect timing.
cards.title: Go Early to the Ossuary

cards.body: The Ossuary and Cathedral of the Assumption can be bought as a combo (120 CZK). The Cathedral is underrated and right next door — don't skip it just because the Ossuary gets all the attention.
cards.title: Buy the Sedlec Combo Ticket

cards.body: The Medieval Mine Tour at the Czech Museum of Silver runs on fixed slots and fills up quickly in summer. Book online at cms-kutnahora.cz, especially for weekends.
cards.title: Book the Mine Tour in Advance

cards.body: The approach to St. Barbara's along the Jesuit College terrace is one of the most scenic walks in Bohemia. Budget 20 min — don't rush past the statues.
cards.title: Walk the Barborská Promenade Slowly

cards.body: Most entrance fees now accept cards, but some smaller sites and local restaurants still prefer cash. Keep 300–500 CZK on you just in case.
cards.title: Bring Cash & Cards

cards.body: Download the Můj vlak app to buy train tickets and check timetables. Mobile tickets are valid on board — no need to print anything.
cards.title: Czech Railways App (ČD / Můj vlak)

tips.note: Kutná Hora is very manageable as a day trip. Last trains back run in the late evening, so there's no need to rush. If you skip the mine tour, you can comfortably see everything else in 6–7 hours.

title: Kutná Hora

transport.callout: Train is the most convenient option from Prague. No car needed — everything in Kutná Hora is walkable once you're there.

steps.text: Take the metro or tram to Praha hlavní nádraží (main station)
Board a direct train to Kutná Hora hl.n. — departs roughly every 2 hrs (07:55, 09:55, 11:55…)
Journey time: ~1 hour
From Kutná Hora hl.n., take bus #1 (2 stops) to Sedlec, or walk ~3 km to the town center
Bus #1 runs from the train station through Sedlec (Ossuary stop) and into the town center
Useful for the first leg of the day — saves a 3 km walk from the station to Sedlec
Check the local timetable at the station as frequency is limited

transport.note: Kutná Hora is very walkable once you're in the center. The main cluster of sites (Italian Court, St. Barbara, Stone House) are all within a 10-minute walk of each other. Sedlec is the only outlier — use the bus or a taxi (~100 CZK) on arrival.

viral.callout: Kutná Hora punches way above its weight for photography. Bone churches, Gothic cathedrals, and cobbled lanes — genuinely eerie and beautiful.
viral.note: Photography is allowed inside the Ossuary (no flash). The Sedlec Cathedral interior is often completely empty — great for wide-angle shots without crowds.

spots.description: The bone chandelier and chandelier coat-of-arms are endlessly photogenic. Go early for fewer crowds and natural window light through the crypt.
spots.title: Sedlec Ossuary Interior

spots.description: The long promenade along the Jesuit College with Baroque statues leading toward the Gothic spires — one of the best walks in the Czech Republic.
spots.title: St. Barbara's Cathedral Approach

spots.description: Panoramic view over the Vrchlice valley from the cathedral terrace. Best in golden hour when the spires glow orange.
spots.title: Valley View from St. Barbara's Terrace

restaurants.callout: Where to eat in Kutná Hora — Bohemian taverns and terraces near the cathedral and the Bone Church, with Google ratings and a couple of viral picks.

places.name: Pivnice Dačický
places.description: Atmospheric beer hall doing Czech classics — svíčková, goulash and tankové pivo.

places.name: V Ruthardce
places.description: Historic restaurant with a terrace looking over the valley toward St Barbara’s.

places.name: Restaurace U Šneka Pohodáře
places.description: Easygoing local pub-restaurant — generous Czech plates without the tourist markup.

restaurants.note: It’s a small town, so lunch is the main event. Pivnice Dačický fills up midday; the terrace at V Ruthardce has the best views over the valley.

budget.totalNote: CZK to EUR ≈ 25:1. Czech Republic uses CZK; Austria uses Euro — bring both or plan to use a card. Ars Electronica tickets can be bought online at ars.electronica.art/center to save time. The Lean variant skips Ars Electronica and the Cubus bar.

eyebrow: Day trip from Prague · Czech Republic & Austria

flights.note: No flights needed — Český Krumlov is a ~3 hour direct bus ride from Prague. Book RegioJet tickets at regiojet.com or via the app. Reserve at least a few days in advance in June — this route fills up fast.

highlights: Český Krumlov Castle — UNESCO-listed baroque complex above the Vltava bend
Castle Tower with the finest panoramic view in South Bohemia
Castle Tour I — Masquerade Hall trompe-l'œil frescoes
Linz Hauptplatz — one of Austria's grandest baroque main squares
Ars Electronica Center — Austria's flagship digital art and tech museum
Linzer Torte at Jindrak — the world's oldest cake recipe, baked since 1929

itinerary.callout: A tight but very doable day: ~3.5 hours in Český Krumlov for the highlights, then ~1.5–2 hours on the bus to Linz for the afternoon. You sacrifice the canoe trip, Baroque Theatre, and Egon Schiele — but you get two countries in one day. Leave Prague no later than 7:00 and book all three bus legs in advance.

days.title: Český Krumlov → Linz
days.subtitle: Czech castle in the morning · Austrian Danube city in the afternoon

items.title: Bus: Praha Florenc → Český Krumlov
items.description: RegioJet departs ~7:00 from Florenc, arrives ~10:00. Book in advance — June fills up fast. Wi-fi, USB charging, snack service on board.

items.title: Castle Gate & Bear Moat
items.description: Quick stop at the entrance to see the brown bears in the original 16th-century moat — a Rožmberk dynasty tradition. Free from the bridge above — 10 min max.

items.title: Castle Tower (Zámecká věž)
items.description: The 162-step climb for the definitive panorama — Vltava river bend, red rooftops, forested hills. The best view in South Bohemia. Worth any queue.

items.title: Castle Tour I — State Rooms
items.description: The main baroque circuit through the Masquerade Hall with its extraordinary trompe-l'œil frescoes. ~50 min guided. The essential castle experience if you're doing only one tour today.

items.title: Quick Lunch in the Old Town
items.description: Hospoda Na Louži or Nonna Gina for a fast, good-value meal. Keep it under 45 min — you need to reach the bus stop by 13:30 at the latest.

items.title: Lazebnický Most & Latrán Wander
items.description: Cross the Barber's Bridge for the classic castle-over-river photo, then stroll Latrán Street toward the bus stop. Your Český Krumlov in a nutshell — then head to the bus.

items.title: Bus: Český Krumlov → Linz
items.description: FlixBus runs a direct connection to Linz Hauptbahnhof — ~1.5–2 hours via the Austrian border. A 13:30–14:00 departure fits perfectly. Check flixbus.com and book ahead — seats are limited.

items.title: Hauptplatz & Landstraße
items.description: From the Hauptbahnhof take Tram 1 or 3 to Hauptplatz (10 min, €2.50). Linz's vast baroque main square is one of the largest in Austria. Walk Landstraße — the pedestrian boulevard — toward the Alter Dom.

items.title: Alter Dom (Old Cathedral)
items.description: The ornate baroque cathedral where Anton Bruckner served as court organist for 12 years. Compact and beautiful inside — free entry, 15 min.

items.title: Ars Electronica Center
items.description: Austria's flagship museum of media art, AI, and digital culture on the Danube riverfront. Walk there from Hauptplatz along the river (10 min). The entire building is a programmable LED display — spectacular at dusk. 45–60 min inside.

items.title: Linzer Torte at Jindrak
items.description: Jindrak is Linz's most famous Konditorei, baking the Linzer Torte (claimed world's oldest cake recipe, dating to 1653) since 1929. On Landstraße — easy to fit in on the way back to the Hauptbahnhof. A Melange and a slice: non-negotiable.

items.title: Bus: Linz Hauptbahnhof → Praha Florenc
items.description: RegioJet runs Linz → Prague in ~3–3.5 hours. Evening departures typically at 18:30, 19:30, or 20:00 from Linz Hauptbahnhof. Book this in advance. Home in Prague by 22:00–23:30.

subtitle: UNESCO Castle, Baroque Rooms & the Danube in One Day
tagline: Two countries in one long day — Czech fairy-tale town in the morning, Austria's design capital by afternoon.

cards.title: Leave Český Krumlov by 13:30
cards.body: This is the critical constraint for the whole day. Aim to be at the Český Krumlov bus stop by 13:15 at the latest to catch a 13:30–14:00 FlixBus to Linz. If you miss this window, Linz becomes a rush. Don't linger over lunch.

cards.title: Book All Three Bus Legs at Once
cards.body: RegioJet Praha → Český Krumlov, FlixBus Český Krumlov → Linz, RegioJet Linz → Praha. Do it all in one session at regiojet.com and flixbus.com. June is busy on every single one of these routes — any missing leg breaks the day.

cards.title: Arrive Early in Český Krumlov
cards.body: The 7:00 RegioJet from Prague gets you to Český Krumlov before 10:00 and before the day-tripper crowds. You get the Castle Tower in peace and Tour I done before midday. Every early minute counts on this itinerary.

cards.title: Skip the Baroque Theatre Today
cards.body: Castle Tour II is spectacular but takes 60+ min and requires pre-booking weeks in advance. There simply isn't time on this route. Save it for a dedicated Český Krumlov overnight trip — it deserves its own day.

cards.title: Linzer Torte is Non-Negotiable
cards.body: Jindrak is on Landstraße, directly on your walking route between Hauptplatz and the Hauptbahnhof. The Linzer Torte with a Melange takes 15 min and costs about €6–8. The world's oldest cake recipe — do not walk past it.

cards.title: Two Currencies — CZK and Euro
cards.body: Czech Republic uses CZK; Austria uses Euro. Bring CZK for Český Krumlov (most places take card, but boat rentals and small shops prefer cash). Bring €40–50 in cash for Linz — Ars Electronica, tram, café, and bus. ATMs at Florenc and Linz Hauptbahnhof.

cards.title: June Weather in Both Regions
cards.body: June can bring afternoon thunderstorms in South Bohemia and Upper Austria. Pack a light rain jacket. Castle Tour I and Ars Electronica are both fully indoors — perfect fallbacks if the weather turns.

tips.note: This route is tight but genuinely rewarding — two very different cities and two countries in one day. If you want to do Český Krumlov properly (canoeing, Baroque Theatre, Egon Schiele), it deserves its own dedicated day trip. Linz also rewards an overnight stay — the Ars Electronica building lit up at night is spectacular, and the city has great food and museum scene.

title: Český Krumlov & Linz

transport.callout: Three bus legs, two countries, one day. Book all three segments at the same time — Prague → Český Krumlov (RegioJet), Český Krumlov → Linz (FlixBus), and Linz → Prague (RegioJet). June is busy on all these routes.

steps.text: Take metro Line B (yellow) to Florenc, then walk 3 min to the Florenc bus terminal
Board RegioJet to Český Krumlov — first departure ~7:00. Take the earliest one.
Journey time: ~3 hours (includes a comfort stop)
Wi-fi, USB charging, and on-board snacks — worth the slight premium over FlixBus

routes.title: FlixBus
routes.subtitle: Praha Florenc / Na Knížecí → Český Krumlov

steps.text: FlixBus departs from Florenc and sometimes Na Knížecí (Anděl metro, Line B)
Slightly cheaper than RegioJet but less frequent and less comfortable
Journey time: ~3 hours
FlixBus runs a direct connection from Český Krumlov bus station to Linz Hauptbahnhof
Aim for a 13:30 or 14:00 departure — earlier is better for maximising Linz time
Journey crosses the Czech–Austrian border — have your ID or passport accessible
Book at flixbus.com well in advance — seats are limited on this route
RegioJet departs Linz Hauptbahnhof — typically at 18:30, 19:30, or 20:00
Journey time: ~3–3.5 hours. Home in Prague by 22:00–23:30
Book at regiojet.com — this is the final leg, don't forget it when booking
Wi-fi, USB charging, and snack service on board
FlixBus also runs Linz → Prague — sometimes cheaper than RegioJet
Check flixbus.com for evening departures that align with your Linz exit time
The entire historic centre is walkable — castle, old town, and bus stop all within 20 min on foot
The castle is a short uphill walk from the bus stop (10 min)
No local transport needed once you're in town

routes.title: Tram + Walking
routes.subtitle: Hauptplatz, Alter Dom & Ars Electronica within easy reach

steps.text: From Linz Hauptbahnhof, take Tram 1 or 3 to Hauptplatz — 10 min, €2.50
Hauptplatz → Alter Dom: 5 min walk south down Landstraße
Alter Dom → Ars Electronica Center: 10 min walk north along the Danube riverfront
Ars Electronica → Jindrak (Landstraße): 12 min walk back toward Hauptplatz
Jindrak → Hauptbahnhof: Tram 1 or 3 back, or 20 min walk

transport.note: Book all three bus legs at the same time: RegioJet Praha→Český Krumlov, FlixBus Český Krumlov→Linz, and RegioJet Linz→Praha. June is busy on all routes. Missing any one connection breaks the whole day.

viral.callout: Two very different cities, two very different aesthetics — baroque riverside perfection in Český Krumlov, futuristic LED-lit Danube architecture in Linz. Both are incredibly photogenic.

spots.title: View from the Castle Tower
spots.description: The iconic panorama — Vltava river bend, red rooftops, forested hills. Nothing else like it in the country. Go up just after opening to beat the queues.

spots.title: Barber's Bridge from the Riverbank
spots.description: Climb down to river level and shoot the bridge with the castle towers rising behind. Golden hour light hits the stone perfectly.

spots.title: Castle from the Water
spots.description: Paddling around the river bend gives you the postcard view of the castle looming above the trees. Only accessible if you rent a boat — worth it for the photos alone.

spots.title: Castle Gardens at Dusk
spots.description: The formal Baroque gardens catch incredible late afternoon light. The cascade fountain and Bellarie summer palace make for clean, elegant compositions.

spots.title: Ars Electronica Center at Dusk
spots.description: The building's entire facade is a programmable LED display — it lights up with dynamic light art installations as evening falls. One of the most striking modern buildings in Austria, and it photographs beautifully reflected in the Danube.

spots.title: Hauptplatz at Golden Hour
spots.description: Austria's largest baroque main square goes golden at dusk. The Dreifaltigkeitssäule (Trinity Column) and surrounding baroque facades photograph beautifully — the late afternoon light makes it.

spots.title: Lentos & Danube Promenade
spots.description: The sleek glass Lentos Kunstmuseum glows red and blue after dark. Walk the riverfront promenade between Lentos and Ars Electronica for the best Linz skyline shots — entirely different from anything in Czech Republic.

viral.note: June is peak season — Český Krumlov crowds hit from 10am onwards. Get there on the first morning bus. In Linz, the Ars Electronica building starts its LED show around sunset — if your bus allows, timing your departure for the 19:30 or 20:00 RegioJet lets you catch the building lit up before you leave.

restaurants.callout: Two cities, two food cultures — Czech medieval grills and riverside spots in Český Krumlov; historic coffee houses, the legendary Linzer Torte, and a Danube-view bar in Linz.

places.name: Krčma v Šatlavské
places.description: Candle-lit medieval tavern grilling meats over open fire — the town's signature meal. Cellar atmosphere, long benches, great beer.

places.name: Laibon
places.description: Riverside vegetarian spot with a terrace right on the Vltava — calm and scenic. Good option if you want to skip the heavier Czech fare.

places.name: Nonna Gina
places.description: Family-run pizzeria — a reliable, fast, good-value break from Czech taverns. Good for a quick lunch when you're on a tight schedule.

places.name: Dobrá čajovna
places.description: Mellow teahouse for a quiet pot of tea away from the old-town crowds. Worth a 20-min stop if you arrive early.

places.name: Pivovar Eggenberg
places.description: The town's historic brewery — fresh Eggenberg lager in a big courtyard hall. Best for a quick beer after Tour I, before catching the Linz bus.

places.name: Jindrak Konditorei
places.description: Linz's most iconic pastry shop, baking the Linzer Torte since 1929. The world's oldest cake recipe — lattice pastry filled with redcurrant jam. Get a slice with a Melange. On your walking route between Hauptplatz and the Hauptbahnhof.

places.name: Café Traxlmayr
places.description: One of Linz's oldest and grandest coffee houses — marble tables, dark wood panelling, formal service, and excellent Viennese-style coffee. A classic Austrian café experience.

places.name: Cubus
places.description: The bar and restaurant at the top of the Ars Electronica Center with sweeping views over the Danube. Great spot for a drink after your museum visit — the LED facade of the building starts lighting up as the evening approaches.

places.name: Gelbes Krokodil
places.description: A well-rated, relaxed Linz restaurant popular with locals — good Austrian and international food, reasonable prices. Solid option if you want a proper sit-down dinner before the evening bus back to Prague.

restaurants.note: In Český Krumlov you're on a tight clock — Nonna Gina is the fastest reliable lunch. In Linz, Jindrak is non-negotiable and sits on your walking route. If you have time to kill before the evening bus, Cubus at Ars Electronica is a great spot for a drink with a view.

title: Kraków
subtitle: Wawel, Kazimierz a najlepšie kaviarne v meste
tagline: Ranný štart cez Tatry, zlaté námestie, obed v Kazimierzi a domov so zásobou spomienok.
highlights: Trasa cez Ždiar a Zakopaný — najkrajšia horská cesta do Poľska
Wawel Castle ráno — nádvorie a výhľad na Wisłu bez davov
Rynek Główny — jedno z najväčších stredovekých námestí v Európe
Kazimierz: street art, kaviarne a najlepší hummus v Poľsku
Charlotte, Padre Coffee, Hamsa, Hevre — top Instagram & TikTok podniky
Späť doma pred 22:00 — žiadne hotelové starosti

itinerary.callout: Plán počíta s odchodom o 7:00 zo Spišskej Belej. Trasa cez Ždiar → Lysá Poľana → Zakopané je krajšia ako diaľničná alternatíva a je podobne rýchla. V Krakove odporúčame zaparkovať raz pri Kazimierzi a chodiť pešo — centrum je malé a plné jednosmeriek.

days.title: Kraków: Wawel → Rynek → Kazimierz
days.subtitle: Od hradného kopca po najlepší flat white v meste

items.title: Odchod zo Spišskej Belej
items.description: Trasa: Spišská Belá → Ždiar → hraničný prechod Lysá Poľana → Zakopané → Kraków. Celkovo ~155 km, asi 2 h 20 min. Na hranici na Lysej Poľane stop zvyčajne do 10 minút. Trasa cez Ždiar je nádherná — Tatry po oboch stranách.

items.title: Wawel Castle & Cathedral
items.description: Zaparkuj pri ul. Dietla (Kazimierz) a vyjdi pešo na Wawelský vrch — 15 minút chôdze. Exteriér a nádvorie sú zadarmo. Katedrála (~10 €) stojí za to — pohrebisko poľských kráľov s kryptou a zlatou kapličkou. Ráno pred 10:00 tu prakticky nie sú turisti.

items.title: Ranná káva: Moment Coffee Roasters
items.description: Ul. Brzozowa 17, Kazimierz. Vlastná pražiareň, výborné espresso a pour-over. Malý a autentický — žiadni turisti, samí locals. Pravidelne sa objavuje na IG krakovskej coffee komunity. Ideálna zastávka po Waweli pred nájazdom na Rynek.

items.title: Rynek Główny — Hlavné námestie
items.description: Najväčšie stredoveké námestie v Európe. Mariánska bazilika (vstup ~8 €, hodinový trúbač je bez vstupného), Sukiennice — historická trhová hala, pohľad na Wieża Ratuszową. Ranné svetlo je tu magické — choď pred 11:00.

items.title: Desiata: Charlotte Bistro & Pain
items.description: Plac Szczepański 2. Francúzska patisserie, ktorá ovládla krakovský Instagram — croissanty s maslom, tartiny so syrom, café au lait. Každý druhý post o Krakove je odtiaľto. Fronta pred otvorením je normálna, príď skôr ako sa Rynek zaplní turistami.

items.title: Presun do Kazimierza
items.description: Z Rynku pešo ~15 minút pozdĺž ul. Grodzka, alebo 5 minút autom. Kazimierz je bývalá židovská štvrť, dnes najživšia gastro a kultúrna časť mesta.

items.title: Obed: Hamsa — Hummus & Happiness
items.description: Ul. Szeroka 2, Kazimierz. Izraelsko-židovská kuchyňa uprostred historickej štvrte — najlepší hummus v Poľsku, shakshuka, pita. Interiér plný detailov, každý záber je Instagramovateľný. Rezervácia cez hamsa.pl odporúčaná — príď pred 12:30 alebo po 14:00.

items.title: Prechádzka Kazimierzom
items.description: Ul. Józefa a Meiselsa — galérie, vintage shopy, street art. Pozri si Starú synagógu (ul. Szeroka 24, múzeum, ~8 €) a Remuh synagógu s histórickým cintorínom. Všetko je v okruhu 5 minút chôdze.

items.title: Popoludňajší drink: Hevre
items.description: Ul. Miodowa 3, Kazimierz. Reštaurácia a wine bar v rekonštruovanej synagóge — vysoké klenby, umelecké svietidlá. Jeden z najphotogenickejších interiérov v Poľsku, dominuje poľskému TikToku. Choď aspoň na pohár prirodzeného vína. Ak nechceš alkohol, Padre Coffee vedľa je taktiež top.

items.title: Záverečná káva: Massolit Books & Café
items.description: Ul. Felicjanek 4, Kazimierz. Legendárna kníhkupecká kaviareň — anglické knihy, domáce koláče, skvelý cheesecake. Obľúbená u locals, takmer žiadni turisti. Ideálna na posledné posedenie pred odchodom domov.

items.title: Posledná prechádzka a nákupy
items.description: Sukiennice na Rynku — ručne vyrobené šperky, jantár, suveníry. Alebo len posedenie na terase s poľským pivom Żywiec pred cestou domov. Odchod ideálne o 18:30 – 19:00.

items.title: Odchod z Krakova — domov
items.description: Odchod o 18:30 – 19:00, doma pred 21:30. Trasa späť rovnaká — Zakopané → Lysá Poľana → Ždiar → Spišská Belá. Odporúčame natankovať v Poľsku pred odchodom — benzín je lacnejší.

transport.callout: Ideálna trasa autom je cez Ždiar a Tatry — krajšia ako severná alternatíva cez Ružomberok a podobne rýchla. Celkovo ~155 km jedným smerom.

routes.title: Spišská Belá → Kraków
routes.subtitle: Cez Ždiar, Lysú Poľanu a Zakopané · ~2 h 20 min

steps.text: Spišská Belá → Ždiar (cez Tatranský národný park, ~25 km, ~30 min)
Ždiar → hraničný prechod Lysá Poľana (~5 km, zvyčajne do 10 min čakania)
Lysá Poľana → Zakopané (~35 km, ~40 min, krásna horská cesta)
Zakopané → Kraków (rýchlostná cesta S7, ~100 km, ~1 h)
Celkom: ~155 km, asi 2 h 20 min bez zastávok

routes.title: Ul. Dietla — Kazimierz (odporúčané)
routes.subtitle: Platené, ale krok od Kazimierza aj Wawelu

steps.text: Pouličné parkovanie na ul. Dietla — platená zóna, platba parkovacím automatom v PLN
Odtiaľ pešo na Wawel: 12 min, do Rynku: 18 min, do Kazimierza: 3 min
V lete miesta nájdeš od 9:00 — príď skôr alebo parkuj v garáži
Krytá parkovacia garáž priamo pod Wawelom
Pešo na Wawel: 5 min, na Rynek: 15 min, do Kazimierza: 10 min
Spoľahlivejšia ako ulica — vždy voľné miesta
Bezplatné parkovisko pri nákupnom centre Bonarka (južná strana mesta)
Autobus č. 103 alebo 173 do centra — ~15 minút, lístok ~0.80 €
Ideálne ak prídeš v špičke a nechceš riešiť parkovanie v centre

transport.note: Benzín v Poľsku (E10/95) je lacnejší ako na Slovensku o ~0.10–0.15 €/l — odporúčame natankovať pri Zakopanom alebo v Krakove pred cestou späť. Mýto na S7 (rýchlostná cesta Zakopané–Kraków) ~1–2 €, platí sa kartou na mýtnej bráne.

viral.callout: Tieto podniky a miesta dominujú na poľskom Instagrame a TikToku. Všetky sú v okruhu 30 minút chôdze — žiadna logistika.

spots.title: Charlotte Bistro & Pain
spots.description: Francúzska patisserie, ktorá doslova ovládla krakovský Instagram. Croissanty s maslom, tartiny so syrom a šunkou, café au lait v miskách. Krásny interiér s výhľadom na námestie. Fronta pred 9:00 je štandard — príď skoro.

spots.title: Padre Coffee
spots.description: Specialty kaviareň v historickom Kazimierzi. Flat white s dokonalým latte artom — záber odtiaľto má každý krakovský travel TikTok. Malý, útulný priestor s tehlovou stenou. Jeden z najlepších espressov v meste.

spots.title: Massolit Books & Café
spots.description: Legendárna kníhkupecká kaviareň s obrovským výberom anglických kníh a domácimi koláčmi. Obľúbená u locals a expats — žiadni turisti. Ideálna na poobedňajší cheesecake a flat white pred cestou domov.

spots.title: Moment Coffee Roasters
spots.description: Vlastná pražiareň so zameraním na pour-over a filter kávu. Minimalistický interiér, pravidelne na IG krakovskej coffee komunity. Nie je to turistické miesto — prídeš sem pre skutočnú kávu.

spots.title: Hamsa — Hummus & Happiness
spots.description: Izraelsko-židovská kuchyňa uprostred historického Kazimierza. Najlepší hummus v Poľsku — s olivovým olejom, sumacom a pitou. Interiér plný detailov v historickej budove. Dominuje na poľskom food Instagrame. Rezervuj vopred na hamsa.pl.

spots.title: Hevre — Wine Bar & Restaurant
spots.description: Reštaurácia a bar v rekonštruovanej historickej synagóge. Vysoké kamenné klenby, umelecké svietidlá, prirodzené vína. Jeden z najphotogenickejších interiérov v Poľsku — dominuje TikToku s miliónovými zábermi. Choď aspoň na pohár vína.

spots.title: Youmiko Sushi
spots.description: Fusion maki a nigiri, ktoré dobyli poľský TikTok food scénu. Dragon roll a tiramisu maki sú viral — každý druhý food video z Krakova je odtiaľto. Dlhšie čakanie, ale oplatí sa.

spots.title: Starka — Restauracja & Wódka Bar
spots.description: Tradičná poľská kuchyňa s rustikálnym interiérom — nakladané zeleniny, pierogi, bigos. Rozsiahla ponuka poľských žitných vodiek. Skvelá atmosféra na večernú zastávku pred odchodom domov.

spots.title: Wawel od rieky Wisły
spots.description: Najikonickejší záber Krakova — pohľad na Wawelský hrad od brehu Wisły. Ráno pred 10:00 tu nie sú davy. Zlatá hodina (ráno alebo podvečer) robí z tohto miesta fotografický raj. Choď pešo od parkoviska Dietla.

spots.title: Ul. Szeroka — srdce Kazimierza
spots.description: Hlavná ulica historickej židovskej štvrte. Stará synagóga, Remuh synagóga s historickým cintorínom, kaviarne a reštaurácie v historických fasádach. Každý krok je fotografia.

spots.title: Kościół Bożego Ciała (Corpus Christi)
spots.description: Gotický kostol s nádherným barokovým interiérom — zlaté oltáre a architektúra, ktorá zaujme každého. Vstup zadarmo, vnútro je fotograficky neuveriteľné. Skrytý klenot, ktorý väčšina turistov minú.

viral.note: Všetky podniky odporúčame vopred skontrolovať na Instagrame — otváracie hodiny sa menia a niektoré sú zatvorené v pondelok. Hamsa a Hevre vyžadujú rezerváciu, zvlášť cez víkend.

flights.note: Cestuješ autom — žiadne lety nie sú potrebné. Trasa Spišská Belá → Kraków cez Ždiar, Lysú Poľanu a Zakopané je ~155 km a trvá asi 2 hodiny 20 minút. Viac detailov v záložke Transport.

rows.details: Tam + späť ~310 km, ~22 l × 1.45 €, delené na 2 osoby
Ul. Dietla (platená zóna) alebo Park & Ride Bonarka (zadarmo)
Moment Coffee alebo Padre — flat white ~18 PLN = ~4.3 €
Hamsa — hummus platter + pita + nápoj (~60–70 PLN)
Charlotte — croissant alebo tartine (~15 PLN)
Exteriér Wawelu zadarmo; Mariánska bazilika ~8 €
Tam + späť, delené na 2 osoby
Krytá garáž Sheraton pri Waweli (celý deň)
Charlotte — croissant, tartine, café au lait (~50 PLN)
Padre Coffee dopoludnia + Massolit popoludní
Hamsa — 2 chody + nápoj (~80–90 PLN/os.)
Hevre wine bar — pohár prirodzeného vína (~35–40 PLN)
Wawel Cathedral + Stará synagóga (~18 €)

budget.totalNote: Ceny v PLN prepočítané kurzom ~4.2 PLN/EUR. Poľsko je cenovo výhodné — obed pre dvoch v Hamse vyjde na ~120–140 PLN (~28–33 €). Mýto na S7 Zakopané–Kraków ~4–6 PLN (~1 €), platí sa kartou.

cards.title: Vyraziť o 7:00 ráno
cards.body: Odchod o 7:00 zo Spišskej Belej = príchod do Krakova pred 10:00. Wawel a Rynek sú ešte pokojné — po 11:00 prichádzajú autobusy s turistami z celej Európy. Ranné svetlo na Waweli od rieky je neuveriteľné.

cards.title: Rezervuj Hamsu a Hevre vopred
cards.body: Hamsa (hamsa.pl) a Hevre sú neustále plné, zvlášť cez víkend. Rezervácia je zadarmo a online — urob ju aspoň 2 dni vopred. Bez rezervácie risknete čakanie 30–45 minút alebo zamietnutie.

cards.title: Karta všade, PLN na drobné
cards.body: 99 % podnikov berie kartu (Visa/Mastercard). Majte aj 100–150 PLN (~25 €) v hotovosti na parkovacie automaty, trhy a prípadné pouličné jedlo. Zmenárne priamo na Rynku majú zlý kurz — vyhýbajte sa im.

cards.title: Natankovať v Poľsku
cards.body: Benzín E10/95 je v Poľsku lacnejší ako na Slovensku o ~0.10–0.15 €/l. Natankujte pri Zakopanom (rýchla zastávka, Orlen pri hlavnej ceste) alebo v Krakove na výjazde. Celkovo ušetríte 2–3 €.

cards.title: Zaparkujte raz a choďte pešo
cards.body: Wawel → Rynek → Kazimierz je celé pešo za 20 minút. Auto zaparkujte pri Kazimierzi (ul. Dietla) a nepoužívajte ho. Centrum je malé, plné jednosmeriek a parkovanie meniť je stratou času.

cards.title: Najlepší záber: Wawel od Wisły
cards.body: Pohľad na Wawelský hrad z brehu Wisły (Bulwary Wiślane) ráno alebo podvečer — to je ten záber, čo vidíte všade. Choďte pred 10:00 alebo po 17:00. Bez turistov, perfektné svetlo, 2 minúty od parkoviska Dietla.

tips.note: Kraków je jedno z mála európskych miest, ktoré prežilo 2. svetovú vojnu takmer nepoškodené — historické centrum je kompletne zachované. Choďte s chuťou blúdiť bez mapy, najlepšie veci nájdete náhodou.

restaurants.callout: Najlepšie miesta na jedlo, kávu a drink v Krakove — overené hodnotenia z Google plus virálne tipy z TikToku a Instagramu. Zoradené podľa hodnotenia a počtu recenzií.

places.name: Miód Malina
places.description: Útulná reštaurácia na Grodzkej — poľská klasika aj cestoviny, vždy plno.

places.name: Pod Aniołami
places.description: Tradičná poľská kuchyňa v stredovekej pivnici — grilované špeciality na bukovom dreve.

places.name: Przystanek Pierogarnia
places.description: Malý bufet s čerstvými pirohmi všetkých druhov — lacné a výborné.

places.name: Massolit Books & Café
places.description: Anglické antikvariát-kaviareň s domácimi koláčmi — ideálne na pokojnú pauzu.

places.name: Karma Coffee Roasters
places.description: Specialty pražiareň blízko centra — jedna z najlepších flat white v meste.

places.name: Wódka Café Bar
places.description: Drobný bar pri Rynku s desiatkami ochutených vodiek — povinná zastávka.

places.name: Forum Przestrzenie
places.description: Kultový bar v bývalom socialistickom hoteli pri Visle — ležadlá a výhľad na Wawel.

restaurants.note: Tip: najviac kaviarní a barov nájdeš v Kazimierzi (židovská štvrť). Na večeru v Pod Aniołami a Miód Malina sa oplatí rezervovať vopred.

title: Slovenia & Italian Coast
subtitle: Ljubljana, Lake Bled, Soča Valley & the Italian Riviera
tagline: Alpine lakes and emerald gorges by day, Adriatic sand and Venetian canals by evening.
highlights: Ljubljana old town, castle and Plečnik's famous bridges
Lake Bled — Vintgar Gorge hike and rowboat to the island church
Vršič Pass & Soča Valley — most scenic drive in the Alps
Piran: Slovenia's Venetian jewel on the Adriatic
Grado island beach resort — fine sand and shallow warm water for kids
Venice day trip — Rialto, San Marco and the Grand Canal

itinerary.callout: ~2,000 km total. Buy a Slovenian e-vignette (€15/week) at evinjeta.dars.si and an Austrian 10-day vignette (€9.90) before you leave. A diesel family car averages ~€180–220 in fuel for the full loop.

days.title: The Long Haul
days.subtitle: Spišská Belá → Ljubljana · ~800 km, ~8 hours

items.title: Depart Spišská Belá at 7 am
items.description: Head west on D1 via Poprad and Bratislava. Fill up before leaving — Slovak fuel is the cheapest of the three countries.

items.title: Coffee & stretch — Bratislava rest stop
items.description: ~3 hours in. Motorway services near Bratislava are decent. Grab coffee and let the kids run around before crossing into Austria.

items.title: Austria: Bratislava → Graz
items.description: Cross the border at Berg/Kittsee. Austrian motorway vignette required (€9.90/10-day, buy at first Austrian petrol station or online in advance).

items.title: Optional lunch stop in Graz
items.description: Graz Hauptplatz is just 5 minutes off the A9 motorway. Great for a 45-minute stretch — grab a sandwich, admire the medieval town hall, then hit the road again.

items.title: Graz → Ljubljana (border at Šentilj)
items.description: Cross into Slovenia near Maribor. Roads are fast and in great condition. Arrive Ljubljana ~5–6 pm. Check in and recover.

items.title: Evening walk on the Ljubljanica River
items.description: After check-in, stroll the riverside promenade. The Old Town is lit up beautifully at night. Dinner at any of the outdoor terrace restaurants on Breg Quay.

days.title: Ljubljana
days.subtitle: Castle, dragon bridges & riverside café culture

items.title: Ljubljana Castle (morning)
items.description: Take the funicular from Krekov trg (€4/adult, €3/child return). Explore the medieval fortress, watchtower and virtual time machine exhibit. Best views of the city from the terrace.

items.title: Dragon Bridge & Triple Bridge
items.description: Jože Plečnik's signature works flank the Old Town. Four bronze dragons guard the Dragon Bridge — kids love it. The Triple Bridge has beautiful café seating on both banks.

items.title: Central Market (Plečnik Colonnade)
items.description: Ljubljana's covered market along the river. Pick up Tolmin cheese, Karst prosciutto, honey and local jams for road-trip snacking. Open Mon–Sat until 2 pm.

items.title: Congress Square & Old Town wander
items.description: The Art Nouveau buildings around Congress Square are photogenic and uncrowded in the afternoon. Let the kids loose on the Prešeren Square fountain.

items.title: Dinner on Stari Trg
items.description: Settle in at a riverside restaurant. Order Štruklji (walnut rolled dumplings), a Kranjska sausage board, and a glass of Rebula white from the Goriška Brda region.

days.title: Lake Bled
days.subtitle: Vintgar Gorge, the island church & legendary cream cake

items.title: Ljubljana → Bled
items.description: 55 km, about 55 minutes on the A2. Leave by 8 am to beat the Vintgar Gorge crowds.

items.title: Vintgar Gorge (8–10 am)
items.description: 1.6 km wooden walkway through a dramatic canyon above the turquoise Radovna river, ending at a waterfall. Entry €10/adult, €6/child. Go early — by 11 am it's packed.

items.title: Pletna boat to Bled Island
items.description: Hire a traditional wooden pletna boat (€18/person round trip) to the only island in Slovenia. Ring the church bell for a wish — a classic family moment. Allow 1.5 hours.

items.title: Bled Castle
items.description: Clifftop medieval castle with the best panorama of the lake. Admission €15/adult, €7/child. The castle terrace photo is the one you came for.

items.title: Kremšnita at Park Café
items.description: Bled's legendary cream cake: vanilla custard and whipped cream between crispy pastry. The original recipe is ONLY at Park Café Bled — €4 each. Non-negotiable stop.

items.title: Bike the lake circuit (optional)
items.description: Rent bikes in Bled village (€8/hour) and cycle the scenic 6 km path around the lake. Flatter than it looks and great for kids 6+.

days.title: Triglav National Park
days.subtitle: Vršič Pass, Soča emerald river & Kozjak Waterfall

items.title: Bled → Vršič Pass → Bovec
items.description: 90 km, ~2 hours. The Vršič road (50 hairpin bends, 1,611 m) is the most dramatic drive in the Alps. Stop at the Russian Chapel (hairpin 8) — a WW1 POW memorial.

items.title: Vršič Pass Summit (1,611 m)
items.description: Pull over at the summit car park. 360° views of Triglav and the Julian Alps. Snow patches visible even in July — kids will love throwing snowballs in midsummer.

items.title: Soča River Trail, Bovec
items.description: Flat easy walk along the famously turquoise-green Soča river. Kids can paddle in the shallows (water is ~14°C — very refreshing in the July heat). 2 km trail.

items.title: Kozjak Waterfall
items.description: 30-minute walk from the roadside car park (€5). The waterfall tumbles into a hidden rock grotto — one of the most cinematic spots in Slovenia. Wear shoes you can get wet.

items.title: Bovec → Portorož (Slovenian coast)
items.description: 120 km, ~2 hours via Koper. Arrive early evening. Check into your beach hotel or apartment in Portorož or nearby Piran.

days.title: Slovenian Coast
days.subtitle: Piran's medieval lanes & Portorož beach

items.title: Piran Old Town (morning)
items.description: Slovenia's most beautiful coastal town — compact Venetian architecture, narrow medieval lanes and gorgeous Tartini Square. Park outside at Fornače (€2/hr) and walk 10 min in.

items.title: St George's Church & City Walls
items.description: Climb to the hilltop church for sweeping views over terracotta rooftops and the Adriatic. Free entry. The 360° panorama from the bell tower is the best in town.

items.title: Portorož Beach (afternoon)
items.description: Portorož is 2 km from Piran — a proper resort beach with sunbeds, lifeguards and calm shallow water ideal for young kids. Sunbed hire ~€8–10/pair/day.

items.title: Dinner in Piran harbour
items.description: Best grilled fish on the Slovenian coast is at the small restaurants around Tartini Square. Try brancin na žaru (grilled sea bass) with Malvazija Istrian white wine (€12/bottle).

days.title: Grado + Venice
days.subtitle: Italian island beach resort & La Serenissima day trip

items.title: Portorož → Grado
items.description: 70 km, ~1.5 hours via Trieste. Cross into Italy near Lipica. Drive along the scenic Gulf of Trieste coast road. Check in to your Grado hotel.

items.title: Grado morning beach
items.description: Grado sits on a sandbar island in its own lagoon — fine white sand, shallow warm Adriatic, perfect for young kids. Beach clubs (stabilimenti) charge €15–20/person for sunbed + umbrella.

items.title: Drive to Venice Mestre (park & ride)
items.description: 1.5 hours north to Mestre. Park at Garage San Giuliano (€7/day, book at asmvenezia.it) then take the ACTV bus 10 minutes across the causeway to Venice island.

items.title: Venice — Rialto & San Marco
items.description: Walk from Piazzale Roma bus terminal, cross Rialto Bridge, reach Piazza San Marco. Take Vaporetto Line 1 back along the Grand Canal. Allow 4–5 hours. Go mid-afternoon to avoid the cruise ship morning crush.

items.title: Gondola ride (optional)
items.description: Official rate €80 for 30 min. Split with another family to bring it to €13–20/person. Book directly with a gondolier at San Tomà or Ferrovia stations, not via hotel concierge.

items.title: Return to Grado
items.description: Leave Venice by 6 pm to avoid causeway traffic. Dinner at a lagoon-side trattoria in Grado — spaghetti alle vongole or frittura mista di pesce.

days.title: Lignano & Drive Home
days.subtitle: Last swim in the Adriatic → 9-hour haul back to the Tatras

items.title: Grado → Lignano Sabbiadoro (optional detour)
items.description: 20 km, 25 minutes. Italy's biggest north Adriatic family resort — 8 km of sandy beach, AquaFun waterpark and relaxed seafood restaurants.

items.title: Final beach morning
items.description: Last swim in the Adriatic. Lignano has great shallow water, beach playgrounds and a fun boardwalk. Let the kids loose while you have a final espresso.

items.title: Seafood farewell lunch
items.description: Order spaghetti alle vongole or grilled branzino at any local trattoria. Budget €15–18/person. Eat by 12:30 pm to be on the road by 1 pm.

items.title: Drive home: Lignano → Spišská Belá
items.description: ~950 km, ~9 hours via Udine → Villach → Salzburg bypass → Vienna → Bratislava → Poprad → Spišská Belá. Two drivers is ideal. Depart 1 pm, arrive ~10 pm.

transport.callout: This is a self-drive road trip — all inter-city travel is by your own car. The routes below cover the key in-city connections at each destination.

steps.text: Walk through Old Town to Krekov trg — about 5 minutes from the Dragon Bridge
Board the funicular at the glass station (runs every few minutes, 9 am–11 pm)
Alternatively, hike the cobblestone path from behind the station — 15 min, free, but steep with small kids

routes.title: Mestre → Venice Island
routes.subtitle: Park at Mestre, bus across the causeway

steps.text: Park at Garage San Giuliano, Mestre — €7/day. Pre-book at asmvenezia.it or just drive in (usually has spaces)
Walk 3 minutes to the bus stop on Viale Stazione
Take ACTV bus line 4 or 5 — runs every 10 minutes, 10–12 min ride across the Ponte della Libertà causeway
Arrive Piazzale Roma — the main Venice bus terminal on the island
Buy ACTV tickets from yellow machines at Piazzale Roma — not from street touts
Line 1: slow scenic route, stops at every landing — best for seeing the Grand Canal
Line 2: fast route, fewer stops — use this to get back to Piazzale Roma quickly
Validate ticket at the yellow gate before boarding. Inspectors do check.
Key stops: Rialto (~10 min), San Marco Vallaresso (~20 min), Accademia (~15 min)

routes.title: Piran → Portorož Beach
routes.subtitle: Old town to the resort beach strip

steps.text: Buses depart from near Tartini Square every 20–30 minutes toward Portorož
Alternatively, walk the 2 km coastal path — about 25 minutes and very scenic
Portorož main beach entrance is right off the main road — hard to miss

transport.note: Ljubljana and Piran are entirely walkable — you won't need a car within either city. In Grado, everything on the island is walkable or cycleable (bike hire €8/day). Lignano has a free tourist shuttle bus running the main beach road all summer.

viral.callout: Slovenia punches way above its weight for camera moments — Lake Bled is genuinely one of the most photogenic places in Europe. Here's where to point the lens.

spots.title: Lake Bled at Sunrise
spots.description: The postcard image of Slovenia: a turquoise mountain lake with a tiny church island and a clifftop castle. Sunrise from Ojstrica viewpoint (20-min hike from the south shore) is worth the early alarm.

spots.title: Vintgar Gorge Walkway
spots.description: A 1.6 km wooden walkway suspended above a turquoise river canyon. The final waterfall only reveals itself at the very end — keeps kids moving the whole way. Go before 9 am for crowd-free shots.

spots.title: Soča River, Bovec
spots.description: The most vividly coloured river in Europe — an almost unnatural emerald green from glacial minerals. The riverside trail puts you right at water level with the colour at its most intense.

spots.title: Piran Rooftop Panorama
spots.description: The view from St George's Church over terracotta rooftops and the Adriatic. Slovenia's most photogenic coastal shot — often mistaken for Dubrovnik or a Venetian island.

spots.title: Venice Grand Canal
spots.description: The Rialto Bridge shot at golden hour is one of the most-posted travel images on earth. Take Vaporetto Line 1 for the slow canal cruise — the light off the palazzos in late afternoon is unbeatable.

spots.title: Grado Lagoon Beach
spots.description: A Roman spa town on a sandbar island surrounded by lagoon. Fine white beach, shallow calm Adriatic — and zero crowds compared to Rimini. The lagoon behind you and open sea in front makes for a great family shot.

spots.title: Lignano Sabbiadoro Sunset
spots.description: Italy's 'Golden Sand' resort faces west along the Adriatic — the sunset here is completely open horizon, no islands or headlands to block it. The boardwalk promenade is classic Italian Riviera.

viral.note: For Lake Bled photography: Ojstrica viewpoint (south shore, 20-min hike) gives the classic island + castle composition. Mala Osojnica is 10 minutes higher for a wider panorama. Both are best at sunrise or in the last 90 minutes of evening light.

lines.label: Route
lines.value: D1 → Bratislava → Vienna → Graz → Ljubljana → Vršič Pass → Bovec → Portorož → Trieste → Grado

lines.label: Route
lines.value: Udine → Villach → Salzburg bypass → Vienna → Bratislava → Poprad → Spišská Belá

flights.note: No flights needed — this is a self-drive road trip. Vignette reminder: buy the Slovenian e-vignette (€15/week) at evinjeta.dars.si BEFORE crossing the border — it's instant and cheaper than at the border. Austrian 10-day vignette is €9.90 at any border petrol station.

rows.details: ~2,000 km loop · diesel ~€1.55/L avg · 7L/100km
6 nights × €70 avg (budget hotels / Airbnb apartments)
7 days × €55/day (mix of supermarket & casual restaurants)
Bled pletna boat, castles, Vintgar Gorge, Venice bus & vaporetto
SI e-vignette €15 + AT vignette €9.90 + misc parking
Piran Fornače, Grado, Mestre Venice park & ride
~2,000 km loop · diesel ~€1.55/L avg
6 nights × €130 avg (3-star hotels with pool or sea view)
7 days × €100/day (sit-down restaurants, local wine nightly)
All castle entries, Bled pletna, gondola ride, Venice day pass
SI + AT vignettes + Grado island road toll (€3)
Covered hotel parking + Venice Mestre + Piran

budget.totalNote: Estimates for a family of 4 (2 adults, 2 children under 12). July is peak season in Bled and on the Slovenian/Italian coast — accommodation prices are at their highest. Book 6–8 weeks in advance, especially for Bled and Portorož.

cards.title: Buy vignettes before you leave
cards.body: Slovenian e-vignette: €15/week at evinjeta.dars.si (instant digital, no sticker needed). Austrian 10-day vignette: €9.90, available online or at any border petrol station. Without them you risk a €150+ fine.

cards.title: Bled: arrive early or go home
cards.body: Vintgar Gorge opens at 8 am — be there by 8:15. The lake and town are swamped by 11 am in July. Staying overnight in Bled lets you see the lake in golden hour light with almost no one around.

cards.title: Vršič Pass: know before you go
cards.body: 50 hairpin bends on a narrow mountain road. Fine for normal family cars — use low gear on the descent. Caravans and motorhomes are officially prohibited. Fill up in Kranjska Gora before you start the climb.

cards.title: Piran parking rule
cards.body: Private cars are banned from Piran old town. Use Fornače car park outside the walls (€2/hour) and walk 10 minutes to Tartini Square. There's no way around this — the gate staff will turn you back.

cards.title: Cash for Italian beaches
cards.body: Grado and Lignano stabilimenti (beach clubs) often charge cash only. ATM queues get long at midday. Withdraw €100 in cash the evening before your beach day to avoid the wait.

cards.title: Beat the July heat
cards.body: Adriatic temps hit 32°C+ in July. Plan water activities (Vintgar Gorge, Soča river paddle) in the cool morning and save city sightseeing for the evening. Venice is brutally hot at midday — go mid-afternoon when the cruise crowds thin out.

tips.note: Grado's lagoon-side beach (Spiaggia dei Gabbiani) is the best for toddlers — ultra-shallow, calm and warmer than the open sea side. Lignano's AquaFun waterpark (€25/adult, €18/child) is a great half-day option if the kids need a change from the beach.

restaurants.callout: Eating from the Julian Alps to the Adriatic — Ljubljana market food, Bled cream cake, Ana Roš in the Soča valley, Piran seafood and Venetian cicchetti. Sorted by rating and review count.

places.name: Klobasarna
places.description: Tiny old-town counter serving Carniolan sausage and ričet stew — fast and authentic.

places.name: Gostilna na Gradu
places.description: Inside Ljubljana Castle — refined takes on regional dishes with local ingredients.

places.name: Odprta Kuhna (Open Kitchen)
places.description: Friday open-air food market (Mar–Oct) with dozens of stalls along the river.

places.name: Cafe Čokl
places.description: Small specialty café in the old town pulling some of the city’s best espresso.

places.name: Daktari
places.description: Eclectic, plant-filled bar near the river — relaxed by day, lively at night.

places.name: Ostarija Peglez’n
places.description: Quirky vintage-styled spot near the lake known for fresh fish and homemade pasta.

places.name: Vila Prešeren
places.description: Lakeshore terrace with direct island views — the postcard lunch spot in Bled.

places.name: Gostilna Pri Planincu
places.description: Long-running tavern doing big portions of Balkan grill and Slovenian classics.

places.name: Park Café — Original Bled Cream Cake
places.description: Home of the original Bled cream cake (kremšnita) — a custard-and-cream must-try.

places.name: Hiša Franko
places.description: Ana Roš’s world-renowned restaurant — a once-in-a-trip alpine tasting menu.

places.name: Topli Val (Hotel Hvala)
places.description: Polished dining room known for Soča trout and Adriatic seafood.

places.name: Gostilna Letni Vrt
places.description: Bovec all-rounder with a leafy garden — solid pizza and local mains after the river.

places.name: Fritolin pri Cantini
places.description: Beloved casual seafood window — order fried fish and calamari at the counter.

places.name: Pri Mari
places.description: Family-run favourite for Adriatic seafood and house-made pasta near the marina.

places.name: Cantina Klet (Pri Brunu)
places.description: Tartini Square hole-in-the-wall pouring cheap local wine with simple sea snacks.

places.name: Trattoria de Toni
places.description: Old-town classic for boreto alla graisana, the local lagoon fish stew.

places.name: Al Canevon
places.description: Cosy trattoria in the lanes doing reliable fried-seafood plates and local wine.

places.name: All’Arco
places.description: Tiny Rialto bàcaro famous for top-tier cicchetti — go at lunch, eat standing.

places.name: Cantine del Vino già Schiavi
places.description: Dorsoduro institution for creative crostini and a glass of wine by the canal.

places.name: Osteria alle Testiere
places.description: Eight-table seafood gem — reserve ahead for some of Venice’s best fish.

places.name: Caffè Florian
places.description: Europe’s oldest café (1720) on Piazza San Marco — pricey, but pure theatre.

places.name: Il Mercante
places.description: Inventive cocktail bar by the Frari church — a step up from the spritz crowd.

places.name: Ristorante Bidin
places.description: Long-standing seafood restaurant — a dependable last dinner before the drive home.

restaurants.note: Hiša Franko (Kobarid) is a destination restaurant — reserve weeks ahead. In Venice, eat cicchetti standing at bàcari and skip the sit-down traps around St Mark’s.

title: Slovenia & the Adriatic
subtitle: Bled → Ljubljana → Piran → Grado → Venice
tagline: Alpine lakes, a Venetian coastal town and warm Adriatic beaches — all from one road trip out of Slovakia.
highlights: Row to the island church on iconic Lake Bled
Hike Vintgar Gorge's emerald canyon in Triglav National Park
Coffee and castle views in Ljubljana's car-free old town
Swim in the Adriatic at Piran's Venetian harbour
Day trip to Venice — canals, Rialto, St. Mark's
Lazy beach afternoons on Grado's shallow lagoon

itinerary.callout: 830 km on Day 1 is the price of admission — leave Spišská Belá by 6 am and you're watching the sun set over Lake Bled. Everything after that is pure reward.

days.title: The Big Drive
days.subtitle: Spišská Belá → Bled, 830 km

items.title: Depart Spišská Belá 6:00 am
items.description: Head west via Poprad and Žilina on the D1. Fill up before crossing into Austria — diesel is €0.25/L cheaper in Slovakia.

items.title: Lunch stop in Graz (~4.5 h in)
items.description: Perfect halfway point. The Hauptplatz has good Austrian Gasthäuser and a shaded square — allow 45 min. It's also where you'll cross into Slovenia an hour later.

items.title: Graz → Bled via A9 & Slovenian A1
items.description: Cross at Šentilj — buy the Slovenian e-vignette at the border shop (~€15/week) or online before you leave. Follow A1 west then A2 north to the Bled exit. ~2.5 h from Graz.

items.title: Arrive Bled — lake walk at sunset
items.description: Check in and walk the 6 km lake loop. The island church silhouette against the Julian Alps is the shot you came for. Low effort after a long drive.

days.title: Lake Bled & Triglav National Park
days.subtitle: Two Slovenian icons in one day

items.title: Bled Castle (8 am opening)
items.description: Perched 130 m above the lake. Get here at opening before coaches arrive — panoramic views over the water and Julian Alps are best in the morning light. Allow 1.5 h.

items.title: Pletna boat to Bled Island
items.description: Traditional wooden gondola from the main dock — €16 pp return. Ring the 99-step church bell for good luck. Leave by 10 am to beat the queue. The boatmen take cash only.

items.title: Vintgar Gorge (afternoon)
items.description: 20 min drive from Bled, park at the trailhead (€5). The 1.6 km boardwalk through the Radovna canyon has emerald-green water under sheer rock faces — genuinely stunning and very family-friendly. Allow 2 h.

items.title: Bohinj Lake swim (optional detour)
items.description: 25 km from Bled — bigger, wilder and far less crowded. Crystal-clear glacial water and no motorboats. Great swap if Bled feels too touristy in the afternoon.

items.title: Dinner: Gostilna Pri Planincu
items.description: Best traditional Slovenian restaurant in Bled — excellent mushroom soup, lake trout and local Laško beer. Book ahead, July fills tables by 7 pm.

days.title: Ljubljana & Drive to the Coast
days.subtitle: Capital city → Piran Riviera

items.title: Bled → Ljubljana (55 km, 45 min)
items.description: Leave by 9 am on the A2. Park in the underground garage near Prešeren Square (€2/h) — old town is pedestrian only.

items.title: Ljubljana Castle via funicular
items.description: €6 pp return from Krekov Square. The viewing tower gives 360° views over the terracotta rooftops and the Kamnik Alps. Worth the 5-min ride.

items.title: Old Town walk: Dragon Bridge → Triple Bridge
items.description: Walk across the iconic Dragon Bridge (copper dragons on each corner), then along the Ljubljanica riverside to the Triple Bridge — Plečnik's masterpiece. The Central Market between them has excellent burek and fresh fruit. The kremna rezina custard slice is the local pastry.

items.title: Coffee at Čajna Hiša, lunch at the market
items.description: Best tea and coffee house in Ljubljana, tucked behind Prešeren Square. Grab market lunch from the covered hall — cheap, local and excellent.

items.title: Ljubljana → Piran (150 km, 1.5 h)
items.description: Leave by 2 pm on H4 then H6. Piran old town is car-free — drive to the Portorož waterfront garage immediately (€1.20/h), it's a 15-min flat walk into town.

items.title: First Adriatic swim, Portorož beach
items.description: Drop bags and walk 5 min to the beach. The Adriatic is 26°C in July, calm and very clear. Well-earned after the drive.

days.title: Slovenian Riviera Day
days.subtitle: Piran old town + Portorož beach

items.title: Piran old town morning walk
items.description: Piran is a tiny Venetian peninsula — no cars, crumbling Gothic campaniles and fishing boats in every alley. Walk the sea walls for the best panoramic view, then climb St. George's Church tower (€2) for a 360° sweep of the Adriatic and Istrian coast.

items.title: Beach day at Portorož
items.description: 5 min from Piran. Portorož has the Slovenian coast's longest sandy beach with proper sunloungers, showers and beach bars. Lucija beach is best for groups — organized, spacious and not as expensive as the resort end.

items.title: Sunset aperitivo on the waterfront
items.description: The promenade cafés in Piran do excellent Aperol Spritz — sit at Café Teater for the best terrace view of the fishing harbour as the sun hits the Adriatic.

items.title: Seafood dinner: Restaurant Neptune
items.description: Grilled branzino, Adriatic shrimp and local Malvazija white wine from the Karst region. Book by phone — July tables fill by 7:30 pm and walk-ins get the worst seats.

days.title: Venice Day Trip & Italian Coast
days.subtitle: La Serenissima + Grado lagoon beach

items.title: Depart Piran 7:30 am → Venice Mestre
items.description: 155 km, ~1.5 h. Park at Garage Comunale Mestre (~€15/day for a minivan). Take tram T1 or bus from Mestre to Venice Piazzale Roma — 12 min, €1.50 pp. Far cheaper than driving onto the island.

items.title: Venice: Rialto → St. Mark's walk
items.description: Cross the Rialto Bridge by 9 am before the selfie-stick crowds. Walk through Campi San Polo and San Marco. St. Mark's Basilica is free entry — queue starts at 9 am, arrive before to walk straight in. Espresso at Caffè del Doge, NOT the piazza cafés (€8 a coffee sitting down).

items.title: Vaporetto Line 1 down the Grand Canal
items.description: €9.50 pp for a 75-min single. Stand at the bow for the best views of the palazzi — Ca' d'Oro, Ca' Rezzonico, and the Rialto from the water. Overpriced but completely unmissable.

items.title: Gelateria Alaska & Frari Church
items.description: Best gelato in Venice: Campo San Giacomo dell'Orio (Gelateria Alaska, cash only). The Gothic Frari Church is 5 min away and has Titian's Assumption over the main altar — worth 20 min.

items.title: Back to Mestre by 1:30 pm → Grado
items.description: 95 km south to Grado, Italy's 'Island of the Sun' — a sand-spit town with warm shallow lagoon beaches. ~1 h drive. Check in and head straight to the beach.

items.title: Afternoon beach & dinner in Grado
items.description: Grado's beach is wide, sandy and the lagoon water stays very shallow for 100 m — great for a relaxed afternoon swim. Dinner at Alla Buona Vite for grilled fish and Friulano white wine.

days.title: Beach Morning & Drive Home
days.subtitle: Grado → Spišská Belá, 890 km

items.title: Final beach morning in Grado
items.description: Last swim and a proper Italian coffee on the seafront. Pack bags the night before — be on the sand by 8:30 am and leave the car park by noon.

items.title: Depart Grado 12:00 noon
items.description: 890 km home: Grado → Udine → A23 north through Tarvisio into Austria → Villach → Graz → Vienna → Bratislava → D1 east → Poprad → Spišská Belá. ETA: 9–10 pm with normal stops.

items.title: Lunch stop in Villach, Austria (~2.5 h in)
items.description: Villach Altstadt has good Austrian Gasthäuser around the Hauptplatz. Allow 45 min for a proper sit-down lunch before the long final stretch through Slovakia.

transport.callout: Pure road trip — one 9-seat minivan for 5 adults is the move. All distances are very manageable. Just leave early on Days 1 and 6 and you'll never feel rushed.

transport.note: Minivan rental (9-seater, e.g. VW Transporter) from Poprad for 6 days: ~€380–450 total, split 5 ways = €76–90 pp. Book 6–8 weeks ahead for July. Europcar and Sixt both have Poprad branches. Austrian vignette: e-vignette.at (€9.90). Slovenian vignette: evinjeta.dars.si (€15). Buy both before departure.

viral.callout: Three countries, one road trip. Here are the six spots that will fill your camera roll — all within a day's drive of each other.

spots.title: Lake Bled
spots.description: A medieval castle on a cliff, a glacial lake and a church on its own island — all backed by snowcapped Julian Alps. One of the most-photographed places in Europe and every bit as good in person. Row out before 9 am in July to get the shot without 40 other boats in it.

spots.title: Vintgar Gorge
spots.description: A 1.6 km boardwalk through an emerald river canyon — the Radovna river runs turquoise green even in peak summer. One of the best easy nature walks in the entire Alps, 20 min from Bled. Arrive before 9 am or it becomes a human conveyor belt.

spots.title: Ljubljana Dragon Bridge
spots.description: Four copper dragons guard the corners of this gorgeous Art Nouveau bridge over the Ljubljanica river. Stands out against the colourful riverside cafés and the castle hill behind. Best shot from the embankment looking east — the dragons glow in the afternoon light.

spots.title: Piran
spots.description: Slovenia's most beautiful town — a tiny car-free Venetian peninsula jutting into the Adriatic. Gothic campaniles, crumbling palazzi, fishing boats and sea walls you can walk around at sunset. Most people don't realise how good it is because they go straight to Croatia.

spots.title: Venice Grand Canal
spots.description: No introduction needed. Take vaporetto Line 1 the full length standing at the bow of the boat — 75 minutes of Baroque and Gothic palazzi sliding past. Go before 10 am: July midday in Venice is 36°C and shoulder-to-shoulder. Morning light on the water is worth every early alarm.

spots.title: Grado Lagoon Beach
spots.description: A long thin island connected to the mainland by causeway — wide sandy beaches, shallow warm lagoon water great for swimming and a well-preserved Roman-Byzantine basilica in the old town. Far calmer than Rimini but only 1.5 h from Venice. The Adriatic's underrated gem.

viral.note: All six spots are on the same road trip route — no detours needed. The morning timing on Bled and Venice is non-negotiable in July: both are genuinely overwhelming by midday.

price.note: Mestre parking (÷5) + tram €1.50 + vaporetto €9.50 + return tram. Fuel for both legs ~€10 pp extra.

budget.totalNote: Mid-range estimate for 5 adults sharing one minivan and apartments. July is peak season in Bled and Piran — book accommodation 2–3 months ahead or prices and availability get painful. Budget variant is very achievable with self-catering breakfasts and market lunches.

cards.title: Book accommodation now — seriously
cards.body: Bled and Piran in July are among the most in-demand spots in Central Europe. Good apartments go 2–3 months ahead. Search Booking.com for 'Bled apartment sleeps 5' and 'Piran old town apartment'. Avoid the big resort hotels in Portorož — overpriced and miles from the best parts of Piran.

cards.title: One 9-seat minivan beats two cars
cards.body: A VW Transporter or Ford Tourneo for 5 adults is far more comfortable and social than splitting into two cars. Europcar and Sixt both have branches in Poprad. Book 6–8 weeks ahead for July. Fill diesel in Slovakia before the Austrian border — it's €0.20–0.30/L cheaper.

cards.title: Buy vignettes online before leaving
cards.body: Austrian motorway vignette: e-vignette.at (€9.90 for 10 days). Slovenian vignette: evinjeta.dars.si (€15 for 7 days). Both are mandatory on the motorways and both can be bought online — no need to queue at a border shop or risk a fine. Do it the day before you leave.

cards.title: Early mornings are the whole game in July
cards.body: Bled island boat queues start at 9 am. Vintgar Gorge fills up by 10 am. Venice midday in July is 36°C and genuinely unbearable with the crowds. The plan: Bled island by 8:30 am, Vintgar by 9 am, Venice by 8:30 am. All three pay off enormously for the 30-minute earlier start.

cards.title: Piran: park in Portorož immediately
cards.body: Piran old town is pedestrian-only — there are very few car spots at the entrance and they're always taken. Drive straight to the Portorož waterfront garage (~€1.20/h). It's a completely flat 15-min walk into Piran along the seafront promenade. Don't waste 20 min circling for a spot.

cards.title: Drink local in every country
cards.body: Slovakia: buy a bottle of Tokaj wine before you leave east Slovakia (Vinotéka in Spišská Belá). Slovenia: Malvazija white wine from the Karst region — order it with the seafood in Piran. Italy/Friuli: local Friulano white in Grado. All three are cheaper and better than imported equivalents on every menu.

tips.note: Five adults in peak July: popular restaurants in Bled and Piran will require reservations. Call ahead the same morning at the latest. The minivan gives you flexibility — if one beach or town is too crowded, you can always move.

restaurants.callout: Bled → Ljubljana → Piran → Grado → Venice, fork-first — cream cake, lake views, Adriatic seafood and Venetian bàcari, ranked by rating and review volume.

places.name: Ostarija Peglez’n
places.description: Quirky vintage-styled spot near the lake known for fresh fish and homemade pasta.

places.name: Vila Prešeren
places.description: Lakeshore terrace with direct island views — the postcard lunch spot in Bled.

places.name: Gostilna Pri Planincu
places.description: Long-running tavern doing big portions of Balkan grill and Slovenian classics.

places.name: Park Café — Original Bled Cream Cake
places.description: Home of the original Bled cream cake (kremšnita) — a custard-and-cream must-try.

places.name: Klobasarna
places.description: Tiny old-town counter serving Carniolan sausage and ričet stew — fast and authentic.

places.name: Gostilna na Gradu
places.description: Inside Ljubljana Castle — refined takes on regional dishes with local ingredients.

places.name: Odprta Kuhna (Open Kitchen)
places.description: Friday open-air food market (Mar–Oct) with dozens of stalls along the river.

places.name: Cafe Čokl
places.description: Small specialty café in the old town pulling some of the city’s best espresso.

places.name: Daktari
places.description: Eclectic, plant-filled bar near the river — relaxed by day, lively at night.

places.name: Fritolin pri Cantini
places.description: Beloved casual seafood window — order fried fish and calamari at the counter.

places.name: Pri Mari
places.description: Family-run favourite for Adriatic seafood and house-made pasta near the marina.

places.name: Cantina Klet (Pri Brunu)
places.description: Tartini Square hole-in-the-wall pouring cheap local wine with simple sea snacks.

places.name: Trattoria de Toni
places.description: Old-town classic for boreto alla graisana, the local lagoon fish stew.

places.name: Al Canevon
places.description: Cosy trattoria in the lanes doing reliable fried-seafood plates and local wine.

places.name: All’Arco
places.description: Tiny Rialto bàcaro famous for top-tier cicchetti — go at lunch, eat standing.

places.name: Cantine del Vino già Schiavi
places.description: Dorsoduro institution for creative crostini and a glass of wine by the canal.

places.name: Osteria alle Testiere
places.description: Eight-table seafood gem — reserve ahead for some of Venice’s best fish.

places.name: Caffè Florian
places.description: Europe’s oldest café (1720) on Piazza San Marco — pricey, but pure theatre.

places.name: Il Mercante
places.description: Inventive cocktail bar by the Frari church — a step up from the spritz crowd.

restaurants.note: Coastal Piran and Venice are busiest midday — eat early. Many Slovenian gostilnas close mid-afternoon between lunch and dinner.
